intro: Al-Tawba — "Repentance" — is the only sūra of the Qurʾān that does not open with the basmala: Qushayrī reads in this absence the sign of a sharp severance from the idolaters, there where the formula of mercy would have stood as the emblem of bond. Medinan, it proclaims dissociation (barāʾa) from those who broke their covenants, unmasks the hypocrites, calls for struggle "with goods and selves" upon the path of God, and reveals the truth of repentance: not mere remorse, but a total return, leaving nothing behind. It culminates with the three companions "left behind" — for whom the earth, vast as it was, became narrow — and who discovered that there is no refuge from God except in Him. The sūra closes on the verse of tawakkul: "God is enough for me; there is no god but He; upon Him I rely; He is the Lord of the immense Throne."
themes: the repentance that returns to God
struggle upon the path of God
the true believers who sell their lives and goods
no refuge from God except in Him

versets.ar: بَرَاءَةٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ إِلَى الَّذِينَ عَاهَدتُّم مِّنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: barāʾatun mina llāhi wa-rasūlihi ilā lladhīna ʿāhadtum mina l-mushrikīn
versets.trad: A declaration of immunity from God and His Messenger to those of the idolaters with whom you had concluded a covenant.
versets.commentaire: The sūra opens without basmala — the formula of mercy withdraws where God pronounces severance. Qushayrī: separation is hard, and the hardest is that which is followed by no return; such is the lot of the idolater, for "God does not forgive that another be associated with Him." This barāʾa falls like a message from the unseen, sudden upon those who had settled into their covenant: the lamp of union, that shone between us, was extinguished by a wind from separation.

mots.def: declaration of dissociation, severance of a bond.

mots.racine: أ ل ه / ر س ل
mots.def: the joint authority that pronounces the rupture.
to enter into a mutual pact.
those who associate others with God.

versets.ar: فَسِيحُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّكُمْ غَيْرُ مُعْجِزِي اللَّهِ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ مُخْزِي الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-sīḥū fī l-arḍi arbaʿata ashhurin wa-ʿlamū annakum ghayru muʿjizī llāhi wa-anna llāha mukhzī l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: "Go through the earth for four months, and know that you cannot thwart God, and that God shall disgrace the unbelievers."
versets.commentaire: God cuts the bond yet grants a reprieve: four months to recover oneself. Qushayrī: if they renounce their straying within this term, they will find at the end what they had lost of union; if they persist, the veil of protection is torn between Him and them. The inner allusion: he who walks toward his own ruin by his own steps has none to blame but himself on the day of regret.

mots.racine: ر ب ع / ش ه ر
خ ز ي / ك ف ر
mots.sens: the One who disgraces the unbelievers

versets.ar: وَأَذَانٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ إِلَى النَّاسِ يَوْمَ الْحَجِّ الْأَكْبَرِ أَنَّ اللَّهَ بَرِيءٌ مِّنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ وَرَسُولُهُ ۚ فَإِن تُبْتُمْ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ وَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّكُمْ غَيْرُ مُعْجِزِي اللَّهِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-adhānun mina llāhi wa-rasūlihi ilā n-nāsi yawma l-ḥajji l-akbari anna llāha barīʾun mina l-mushrikīna wa-rasūluh, fa-in tubtum fa-huwa khayrun lakum, wa-in tawallaytum fa-ʿlamū annakum ghayru muʿjizī llāh, wa-bashshiri lladhīna kafarū bi-ʿadhābin alīm
versets.trad: And a proclamation from God and His Messenger unto mankind on the day of the Great Pilgrimage: "God and His Messenger disavow the idolaters. If you repent, it will be better for you; but if you turn away, know that you cannot thwart God." Announce to those who disbelieve a painful chastisement,
versets.commentaire: The Hajj becomes the stage of a proclamation: they were allies, now they are enemies. Qushayrī broadens: whoever sees in any created thing an autonomous effect, without referring it to the divine decree, has in truth associated — and has earned this barāʾa. Yet the door of repentance remains open: if they return to the threshold, God does not sever their hope; He extends the term until every excuse is removed.

mots.racine: ح ج ج / ك ب ر
mots.sens: the day of the Great Pilgrimage

mots.def: ironic here: to announce a chastisement.

versets.ar: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ عَاهَدتُّم مِّنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَنقُصُوكُمْ شَيْئًا وَلَمْ يُظَاهِرُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَحَدًا فَأَتِمُّوا إِلَيْهِمْ عَهْدَهُمْ إِلَىٰ مُدَّتِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: illā lladhīna ʿāhadtum mina l-mushrikīna thumma lam yanquṣūkum shayʾan wa-lam yuẓāhirū ʿalaykum aḥadan fa-atimmū ilayhim ʿahdahum ilā muddatihim, inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muttaqīn
versets.trad: Except those idolaters with whom you have concluded a pact and who have not since broken it, nor lent support to any against you: fulfil the pact with them to its appointed term. Truly God loves those who are mindful of Him.
versets.commentaire: The edge of barāʾa stops where faithfulness remains. Qushayrī: whoever fulfils his word, add for him to the keeping of your covenant; for the faithful and the perjurer are not alike. Taqwā is no mere fear but that uprightness which makes one worthy of being loved: the godfearing is he who fears his own self-love, leaving his portion to fulfil the right of his Lord.

mots.sens: they have not diminished your right

mots.def: those who guard themselves through reverent awe.

versets.ar: فَإِذَا انسَلَخَ الْأَشْهُرُ الْحُرُمُ فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ حَيْثُ وَجَدتُّمُوهُمْ وَخُذُوهُمْ وَاحْصُرُوهُمْ وَاقْعُدُوا لَهُمْ كُلَّ مَرْصَدٍ ۚ فَإِن تَابُوا وَأَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتَوُا الزَّكَاةَ فَخَلُّوا سَبِيلَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-idhā nsalakha l-ashhuru l-ḥurumu fa-qtulū l-mushrikīna ḥaythu wajadtumūhum wa-khudhūhum wa-ḥṣurūhum wa-qʿudū lahum kulla marṣad, fa-in tābū wa-aqāmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātawū z-zakāta fa-khallū sabīlahum, inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: When the sacred months have passed, slay the idolaters wherever you find them; seize them, besiege them, and lie in wait for them at every place of ambush. But if they repent, perform the prayer and give the alms, leave their way free: truly God is Forgiving, Merciful.
versets.commentaire: What has been called the "sword verse" turns at once into the door of pardon. Qushayrī: the truth of repentance is the total return, leaving nothing behind; when the enemy submits, the way is set free. And the sūra opens the inner path: your most implacable enemy is the self lodged between your two flanks; the great struggle is that — to lead it by the rigour of disciplines, ever taking the harder way.

mots.racine: ش ه ر / ح ر م
خ ل و / س ب ل

versets.ar: وَإِنْ أَحَدٌ مِّنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ اسْتَجَارَكَ فَأَجِرْهُ حَتَّىٰ يَسْمَعَ كَلَامَ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ أَبْلِغْهُ مَأْمَنَهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in aḥadun mina l-mushrikīna stajāraka fa-ajirhu ḥattā yasmaʿa kalāma llāhi thumma ablighhu maʾmanah, dhālika bi-annahum qawmun lā yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: If any of the idolaters asks you for sanctuary, grant it to him so that he may hear the Word of God; then convey him to his place of safety: that is because they are a people who do not know.
versets.commentaire: In the very heart of severance, an opening of mercy: sanctuary is owed to whoever asks for it, until the Word reaches him. Qushayrī: if the enemy obtains this right of hearing, what then of the believer who asks sanctuary all his life from any severance? How could he ever be hindered from hearing the Word? And if such is the generosity to one who knows not, what shall be the generosity to him who knows?

versets.ar: كَيْفَ يَكُونُ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ عَهْدٌ عِندَ اللَّهِ وَعِندَ رَسُولِهِ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ عَاهَدتُّمْ عِندَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ ۖ فَمَا اسْتَقَامُوا لَكُمْ فَاسْتَقِيمُوا لَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: kayfa yakūnu li-l-mushrikīna ʿahdun ʿinda llāhi wa-ʿinda rasūlihi illā lladhīna ʿāhadtum ʿinda l-masjidi l-ḥarām, fa-mā staqāmū lakum fa-staqīmū lahum, inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muttaqīn
versets.trad: How can there be a covenant before God and His Messenger for the idolaters, save those with whom you sealed a pact at the Sacred Mosque? So long as they remain true to you, be true to them. Truly God loves those who are mindful of Him.
versets.commentaire: Faithfulness calls faithfulness; deviance calls severance. Qushayrī: how should one impoverished in knowledge be the equal of the sincere in faith? How should one veiled from contemplation be like one annihilated in His presence? How should he who says "I" be like him who says "Thou"? The upright (mustaqīm) is he who fears to love his ego — and gives back to the Lord what is His.

mots.racine: س ج د / ح ر م

mots.def: to be true to one's pledge.

versets.ar: كَيْفَ وَإِن يَظْهَرُوا عَلَيْكُمْ لَا يَرْقُبُوا فِيكُمْ إِلًّا وَلَا ذِمَّةً ۚ يُرْضُونَكُم بِأَفْوَاهِهِمْ وَتَأْبَىٰ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ فَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: kayfa wa-in yaẓharū ʿalaykum lā yarqubū fīkum illan wa-lā dhimmatan, yurḍūnakum bi-afwāhihim wa-taʾbā qulūbuhum wa-aktharuhum fāsiqūn
versets.trad: How [could there be a pact]? — for if they prevail over you, they will respect neither kinship nor pledge of protection. They please you with their mouths, but their hearts refuse, and most of them are corrupt.
themes: the split between mouth and heart
versets.commentaire: The visible lies; the invisible speaks. Qushayrī here paints the hypocrite: they live with you in the garb of concord, yet conceal within them the very source of discord and the blackness of hypocrisy. And he draws an opposite portrait: the noble one, when he prevails, forgives; when he can harm, does not betray — neither in secret nor in the open.

mots.racine: أ ب ي / ق ل ب

versets.ar: اشْتَرَوْا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا فَصَدُّوا عَن سَبِيلِهِ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ سَاءَ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: ishtarū bi-āyāti llāhi thamanan qalīlan fa-ṣaddū ʿan sabīlih, innahum sāʾa mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: They have sold the signs of God for a paltry price, and have turned others from His path. Evil indeed is what they have done.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who accepts from God anything other than God has cheapened his transaction; he has lost in his trade. Far from God, he tastes nothing in lieu of Him; short of God, he finds nothing to content him. He is severed from God, without even the enjoyment of a substitute — this is the manifest loss.

mots.racine: ث م ن / ق ل ل

versets.ar: لَا يَرْقُبُونَ فِي مُؤْمِنٍ إِلًّا وَلَا ذِمَّةً ۚ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُعْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: lā yarqubūna fī muʾminin illan wa-lā dhimma, wa-ūlāʾika humu l-muʿtadūn
versets.trad: They respect neither kinship nor pledge toward any believer; such are the transgressors.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: how would he who does not honour the right of God in God honour the right of the believers? Their natures resemble in the breach of every inviolability. The transgressor (muʿtadī) is he who oversteps the limit (ḥadd) — the unbridled soul that recognises neither Lord nor brother.

versets.ar: فَإِن تَابُوا وَأَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتَوُا الزَّكَاةَ فَإِخْوَانُكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ ۗ وَنُفَصِّلُ الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-in tābū wa-aqāmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātawū z-zakāta fa-ikhwānukum fī d-dīn, wa-nufaṣṣilu l-āyāti li-qawmin yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: But if they repent, perform the prayer and give the alms, they are your brethren in religion. We make the signs distinct for a people who know.
versets.commentaire: Three seals open brotherhood: return, prayer, almsgiving. Qushayrī: if they are admitted to our covenant, then the bond of blood in religion ties you to them; if not, let strangers remain on your margins. Brotherhood is not of origin but of belonging to the Real.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
أ ت ي / ز ك و
أ خ و / د ي ن

versets.ar: وَإِن نَّكَثُوا أَيْمَانَهُم مِّن بَعْدِ عَهْدِهِمْ وَطَعَنُوا فِي دِينِكُمْ فَقَاتِلُوا أَئِمَّةَ الْكُفْرِ ۙ إِنَّهُمْ لَا أَيْمَانَ لَهُمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَنتَهُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in nakathū aymānahum min baʿdi ʿahdihim wa-ṭaʿanū fī dīnikum fa-qātilū aʾimmata l-kufri, innahum lā aymāna lahum laʿallahum yantahūn
versets.trad: But if they break their oaths after their covenant, and revile your religion, then fight the leaders of disbelief — they have no [binding] oaths — that they may desist.
versets.commentaire: Strike the evil at its head. Qushayrī: aim where the millstone of sedition turns, where the branch of evil springs from its root; these are the masters and ringleaders of the unbelievers. The truth of combat is to prepare strength outwardly while inwardly disowning all strength and power of one's own.

mots.def: nakatha: to undo what was tied.

mots.racine: ط ع ن / د ي ن
أ م م / ك ف ر

versets.ar: أَلَا تُقَاتِلُونَ قَوْمًا نَّكَثُوا أَيْمَانَهُمْ وَهَمُّوا بِإِخْرَاجِ الرَّسُولِ وَهُم بَدَءُوكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ۚ أَتَخْشَوْنَهُمْ ۚ فَاللَّهُ أَحَقُّ أَن تَخْشَوْهُ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: a-lā tuqātilūna qawman nakathū aymānahum wa-hammū bi-ikhrāji r-rasūli wa-hum badaʾūkum awwala marra, a-takhshawnahum, fa-llāhu aḥaqqu an takhshawhu in kuntum muʾminīn
versets.trad: Will you not fight a people who broke their oaths, plotted to expel the Messenger, and attacked you first? Do you fear them? God has more right to be feared, if you are believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two fears: the fear of God is the herald of union; the fear of any other than God, the harbinger of separation. True fear is what turns the inmost self from breaching the prohibition and disobeying the command. Fight by obedience to the command, not by personal resentment: he who is angered for himself is blameworthy; he who is angered for God — the help of God is near.

mots.racine: ه م م / خ ر ج

versets.ar: قَاتِلُوهُمْ يُعَذِّبْهُمُ اللَّهُ بِأَيْدِيكُمْ وَيُخْزِهِمْ وَيَنصُرْكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ وَيَشْفِ صُدُورَ قَوْمٍ مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: qātilūhum yuʿadhdhibhumu llāhu bi-aydīkum wa-yukhzihim wa-yanṣurkum ʿalayhim wa-yashfi ṣudūra qawmin muʾminīn
versets.trad: Fight them! God will chastise them through your hands, disgrace them, give you victory over them, and heal the breasts of believing people,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens a spectrum: the healing of believers' breasts varies according to their degrees. For some, it lies in the defeat of the enemy; for others, in attaining the longed-for; for others still, in meeting the Beloved; and for the highest, in subsisting in their very Adored.

mots.racine: ش ف ي / ص د ر

versets.ar: وَيُذْهِبْ غَيْظَ قُلُوبِهِمْ ۗ وَيَتُوبُ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَاءُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-yudhhib ghayẓa qulūbihim, wa-yatūbu llāhu ʿalā man yashāʾu, wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīm
versets.trad: and remove the anger of their hearts. God turns in mercy to whom He wills; God is Knowing, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the removal of anger from hearts has many causes and opens many doors; in what we mention there is a sign of what we leave unsaid. And he sets this pearl: "He turns to whom He wills" — thus the creature's autonomy fades before the One who transforms states.

mots.racine: ذ ه ب / غ ي ظ

mots.def: tāba ʿalā: to accept the return.

versets.ar: أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَن تُتْرَكُوا وَلَمَّا يَعْلَمِ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ جَاهَدُوا مِنكُمْ وَلَمْ يَتَّخِذُوا مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَلَا رَسُولِهِ وَلَا الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَلِيجَةً ۚ وَاللَّهُ خَبِيرٌ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: am ḥasibtum an tutrakū wa-lammā yaʿlami llāhu lladhīna jāhadū minkum wa-lam yattakhidhū min dūni llāhi wa-lā rasūlihi wa-lā l-muʾminīna walījatan, wa-llāhu khabīrun bi-mā taʿmalūn
versets.trad: Did you suppose you would be left, while God does not yet know those of you who have striven and have taken no intimate confidant apart from God, His Messenger, and the believers? God is well acquainted with what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever thinks that mere claim will be accepted without the realization of meaning is mistaken in his reckoning. What is demanded is truthful striving in God, the abandonment of every prop other than Him, distance from intimacy with His enemies — by trust in Him, sufficiency in Him, disengagement from all but Him. The first enemy to exile, he says, is the self itself, the most implacable of foes.

versets.ar: مَا كَانَ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ أَن يَعْمُرُوا مَسَاجِدَ اللَّهِ شَاهِدِينَ عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِهِم بِالْكُفْرِ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَالُهُمْ وَفِي النَّارِ هُمْ خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: mā kāna li-l-mushrikīna an yaʿmurū masājida llāhi shāhidīna ʿalā anfusihim bi-l-kufri, ūlāʾika ḥabiṭat aʿmāluhum wa-fī n-nāri hum khālidūn
versets.trad: It is not for the idolaters to maintain God's places of worship while bearing witness against themselves to disbelief. Their works are vain, and in the Fire they shall abide.
versets.commentaire: To maintain mosques is to maintain worship; worship is accepted only through sincerity. Qushayrī widens: whoever, in his heart, ascribes any independent power to other than God in fact partakes in association; he draws this same barāʾa upon himself.

mots.racine: ع م ر / س ج د

mots.sens: their works have come to nothing
mots.def: ḥabaṭa: to be brought to naught.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا يَعْمُرُ مَسَاجِدَ اللَّهِ مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَأَقَامَ الصَّلَاةَ وَآتَى الزَّكَاةَ وَلَمْ يَخْشَ إِلَّا اللَّهَ ۖ فَعَسَىٰ أُولَٰئِكَ أَن يَكُونُوا مِنَ الْمُهْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: innamā yaʿmuru masājida llāhi man āmana bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-aqāma ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātā z-zakāta wa-lam yakhsha illā llāha, fa-ʿasā ūlāʾika an yakūnū mina l-muhtadīn
versets.trad: Only he shall maintain God's places of worship who believes in God and the Last Day, performs the prayer, gives the alms, and fears none but God. It may be that these will be among the rightly guided.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the mosques are populated only by laying waste the homelands of the human: the adorer populates them by laying waste the homeland of desire; the renunciant, by laying waste the homeland of his will; the gnostic, by laying waste the homeland of his attachments; the one of tawḥīd, by laying waste even the homeland of his own gazing and dwelling with self.

versets.ar: أَجَعَلْتُمْ سِقَايَةَ الْحَاجِّ وَعِمَارَةَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ كَمَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَجَاهَدَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۚ لَا يَسْتَوُونَ عِندَ اللَّهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: a-jaʿaltum siqāyata l-ḥājji wa-ʿimārata l-masjidi l-ḥarāmi ka-man āmana bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-jāhada fī sabīli llāh, lā yastawūna ʿinda llāh, wa-llāhu lā yahdī l-qawma ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: Do you reckon the giving of drink to pilgrims and the upkeep of the Sacred Mosque as equal to him who believes in God and the Last Day and strives in the way of God? They are not equal in God's sight. God does not guide a people of wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the disparity: he who acts outwardly is not he who stands firm in the continuity of the secret; he who lights himself by the lamp of his knowledge is not he who sees by the suns of his gnosis; he who serves at the door is not he who is admitted to the carpet of nearness.

mots.racine: س ق ي / ح ج ج
mots.def: siqāya: the act of providing water.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَهَاجَرُوا وَجَاهَدُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ بِأَمْوَالِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ أَعْظَمُ دَرَجَةً عِندَ اللَّهِ ۚ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْفَائِزُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna āmanū wa-hājarū wa-jāhadū fī sabīli llāhi bi-amwālihim wa-anfusihim aʿẓamu darajatan ʿinda llāh, wa-ūlāʾika humu l-fāʾizūn
versets.trad: Those who have believed, emigrated, and striven in the way of God with their wealth and their persons hold a greater degree in God's sight: such are the triumphant!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī comments each word: "they believed" — they saw by the lights of their intuition, until no cloud of doubt remained in the sky of their certainty; "they emigrated" — they halted in no homeland of dispersion; "they strove" — without regard to recompense, they preferred the Real to themselves, and found grace in subsisting through Him after passing away from the creature.

mots.racine: م و ل / ن ف س

versets.ar: يُبَشِّرُهُمْ رَبُّهُم بِرَحْمَةٍ مِّنْهُ وَرِضْوَانٍ وَجَنَّاتٍ لَّهُمْ فِيهَا نَعِيمٌ مُّقِيمٌ
versets.tr: yubashshiruhum rabbuhum bi-raḥmatin minhu wa-riḍwānin wa-jannātin lahum fīhā naʿīmun muqīm
versets.trad: Their Lord gives them glad tidings of a mercy from Himself, of His good pleasure, and of Gardens wherein there is enduring delight,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: glad tidings to the sinner are mercy, to the obedient God's good pleasure, to all the Garden. But what surpasses every tiding is the meeting: one people has its felicity in their Lord's gift in perfection; another, in their Lord's encounter in perpetuity.

mots.racine: ن ع م / ق و م

versets.ar: خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عِندَهُ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: khālidīna fīhā abadan, inna llāha ʿindahu ajrun ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: abiding therein for ever. With God lies a magnificent reward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "abiding therein" — the pronoun may refer to the Garden, or to the very state: just as His grace is not severed in the Garden, His vision is not denied them whenever they wish in the Garden. "Neither cut off nor withheld" — His bounty not severed, His sight not withheld.

mots.racine: أ ج ر / ع ظ م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَّخِذُوا آبَاءَكُمْ وَإِخْوَانَكُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَ إِنِ اسْتَحَبُّوا الْكُفْرَ عَلَى الْإِيمَانِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّهُم مِّنكُمْ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tattakhidhū ābāʾakum wa-ikhwānakum awliyāʾa ini staḥabbū l-kufra ʿalā l-īmān, wa-man yatawallahum minkum fa-ūlāʾika humu ẓ-ẓālimūn
versets.trad: O you who believe, take not your fathers and brothers as intimates if they prefer disbelief to faith. Those of you who take them as intimates are the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who by his obedience is not worthy of his Lord, take him not as intimate of yourself. Blood does not save the one who distances himself from the Real; he who has preferred something to God is only blessed insofar as he severs it for Him.

versets.ar: قُلْ إِن كَانَ آبَاؤُكُمْ وَأَبْنَاؤُكُمْ وَإِخْوَانُكُمْ وَأَزْوَاجُكُمْ وَعَشِيرَتُكُمْ وَأَمْوَالٌ اقْتَرَفْتُمُوهَا وَتِجَارَةٌ تَخْشَوْنَ كَسَادَهَا وَمَسَاكِنُ تَرْضَوْنَهَا أَحَبَّ إِلَيْكُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَجِهَادٍ فِي سَبِيلِهِ فَتَرَبَّصُوا حَتَّىٰ يَأْتِيَ اللَّهُ بِأَمْرِهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: qul in kāna ābāʾukum wa-abnāʾukum wa-ikhwānukum wa-azwājukum wa-ʿashīratukum wa-amwālun iqtaraftumūhā wa-tijāratun takhshawna kasādahā wa-masākinu tarḍawnahā aḥabba ilaykum mina llāhi wa-rasūlihi wa-jihādin fī sabīlih, fa-tarabbaṣū ḥattā yaʾtiya llāhu bi-amrih, wa-llāhu lā yahdī l-qawma l-fāsiqīn
versets.trad: Say: "If your fathers, sons, brothers, wives, kinsfolk, the wealth you have acquired, a commerce whose stagnation you fear, and dwellings in which you delight, are dearer to you than God, His Messenger, and striving in His path, then wait until God brings His command." God does not guide a corrupt people.
versets.commentaire: Eight attachments — ascendants, descendants, collaterals, spouses, clan, wealth, trade, dwellings — to measure real love. Qushayrī: the sign of sincerity in tawḥīd is to cut attachments, to leave custom, to abandon habit, and to be content with God in the length of states. And: when your religion's market stalls, the markets of your pleasures stall.

versets.ar: لَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللَّهُ فِي مَوَاطِنَ كَثِيرَةٍ ۙ وَيَوْمَ حُنَيْنٍ ۙ إِذْ أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ كَثْرَتُكُمْ فَلَمْ تُغْنِ عَنكُمْ شَيْئًا وَضَاقَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْأَرْضُ بِمَا رَحُبَتْ ثُمَّ وَلَّيْتُم مُّدْبِرِينَ
versets.tr: laqad naṣarakumu llāhu fī mawāṭina kathīratin wa-yawma ḥunaynin idh aʿjabatkum kathratukum fa-lam tughni ʿankum shayʾan wa-ḍāqat ʿalaykumu l-arḍu bi-mā raḥubat thumma wallaytum mudbirīn
versets.trad: God has helped you on many fields and on the day of Ḥunayn, when your great number pleased you yet availed you nothing, when the earth, broad as it was, became too narrow for you, and you turned back in flight.
versets.commentaire: Ḥunayn: lesson of self-complacency. Qushayrī: victory comes from God through the contemplation of Power; the supported is the one whom God preserves from illusion and reckoning, whom He does not consign to his own management, and whom He fixes in the rank of need — exempt from any power or claim of his own.

mots.racine: ع ج ب / ك ث ر
ض ي ق / أ ر ض
mots.sens: the earth was narrow upon you

versets.ar: ثُمَّ أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ سَكِينَتَهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِ وَعَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَأَنزَلَ جُنُودًا لَّمْ تَرَوْهَا وَعَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَاءُ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: thumma anzala llāhu sakīnatahu ʿalā rasūlihi wa-ʿalā l-muʾminīna wa-anzala junūdan lam tarawhā wa-ʿadhdhaba lladhīna kafarū, wa-dhālika jazāʾu l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: Then God sent down His Sakīna upon His Messenger and the believers, and sent down armies you did not see, and chastised those who disbelieved: such is the recompense of the unbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defines: the Sakīna is the coolness of the heart when the Lord's decree passes through; it is quietude, the extinction of the traces of self, contentment with what surges from the unseen, without any protest of choice. And he adds: it is repose upon the carpet of witnessing, the upholding of the qualities of servanthood, without anything in the being stirring against the decree.

mots.racine: ج ن د / ر أ ي

versets.ar: ثُمَّ يَتُوبُ اللَّهُ مِن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَاءُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: thumma yatūbu llāhu min baʿdi dhālika ʿalā man yashāʾu, wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: Then, after that, God turns in mercy to whom He wills. God is Forgiving, Merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He brings them back from ignorance to the realities of knowledge, then carries them from these stations to the contemplations of certainty, then raises them above all this by what He bestows on them: the very source of the Gathering.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْمُشْرِكُونَ نَجَسٌ فَلَا يَقْرَبُوا الْمَسْجِدَ الْحَرَامَ بَعْدَ عَامِهِمْ هَٰذَا ۚ وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ عَيْلَةً فَسَوْفَ يُغْنِيكُمُ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ إِن شَاءَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū innamā l-mushrikūna najasun fa-lā yaqrabū l-masjida l-ḥarāma baʿda ʿāmihim hādhā, wa-in khiftum ʿaylatan fa-sawfa yughnīkumu llāhu min faḍlihi in shāʾ, inna llāha ʿalīmun ḥakīm
versets.trad: O you who believe, the idolaters are nothing but impurity: let them not approach the Sacred Mosque after this year of theirs. If you fear poverty, God will enrich you from His bounty, if He wills. God is Knowing, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they lost the purity of secrets in the water of tawḥīd, and remained in the soilings of conjecture and illusion; they were thus forbidden access to the mosques, which are the very stations of nearness. And: he who halts at the courtyard of the Master's generosity, who invokes the rain of His munificence, is dispensed from every cause and every weariness; every request and need is granted him without even asking.

versets.ar: قَاتِلُوا الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَلَا بِالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَلَا يُحَرِّمُونَ مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَلَا يَدِينُونَ دِينَ الْحَقِّ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ حَتَّىٰ يُعْطُوا الْجِزْيَةَ عَن يَدٍ وَهُمْ صَاغِرُونَ
versets.tr: qātilū lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bi-llāhi wa-lā bi-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-lā yuḥarrimūna mā ḥarrama llāhu wa-rasūluhu wa-lā yadīnūna dīna l-ḥaqqi mina lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba ḥattā yuʿṭū l-jizyata ʿan yadin wa-hum ṣāghirūn
versets.trad: Fight those of the People of the Book who do not believe in God or the Last Day, who do not forbid what God and His Messenger have forbidden, and do not practice the religion of truth, until they pay the tribute with their own hand in submission.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī moves at once toward the inner combat: your most violent foe, the furthest from faith, is the soul fashioned for evil — it does not surrender except to the blade of the great strivings. It does not believe in the decree, its doubt never lifts; it clings to self-management, settles only at the sight of the tangible, accepts from you only the false promise. Hence the ancient saying: "Belie the soul when it speaks to you, for the truthful word drives away hope."

mots.racine: د ي ن / ح ق ق
mots.sens: do not practice the religion of truth

mots.def: those who accept the lower status.

versets.ar: وَقَالَتِ الْيَهُودُ عُزَيْرٌ ابْنُ اللَّهِ وَقَالَتِ النَّصَارَى الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ اللَّهِ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ قَوْلُهُم بِأَفْوَاهِهِمْ ۖ يُضَاهِئُونَ قَوْلَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِن قَبْلُ ۚ قَاتَلَهُمُ اللَّهُ ۚ أَنَّىٰ يُؤْفَكُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālati l-yahūdu ʿuzayrun ibnu llāhi wa-qālati n-naṣārā l-masīḥu ibnu llāh, dhālika qawluhum bi-afwāhihim, yuḍāhiʾūna qawla lladhīna kafarū min qabl, qātalahumu llāh, annā yuʾfakūn
versets.trad: The Jews say Ezra is the son of God; the Christians say the Messiah is the son of God. Such is the word of their mouths, conforming to the saying of those who disbelieved before them. May God afflict them! How perverted they are!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the unbelievers before them denied Lordship; these recognised it, then when they ascribed to Him a son, they undid what they had affirmed of tawḥīd, and became like the first. And: whoever ascribes to the Adored what His transcendence excludes becomes brother to the unbelievers in deserving regret and reproach.

versets.ar: اتَّخَذُوا أَحْبَارَهُمْ وَرُهْبَانَهُمْ أَرْبَابًا مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَالْمَسِيحَ ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَمَا أُمِرُوا إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُوا إِلَٰهًا وَاحِدًا ۖ لَّا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ سُبْحَانَهُ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: ittakhadhū aḥbārahum wa-ruhbānahum arbāban min dūni llāhi wa-l-masīḥa bna maryam, wa-mā umirū illā li-yaʿbudū ilāhan wāḥidā, lā ilāha illā huw, subḥānahu ʿammā yushrikūn
versets.trad: They have taken their rabbis and monks as lords besides God, and the Messiah son of Mary, though they were commanded to worship but One God. There is no god but He: Glory be to Him above what they associate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever sees in a creature a particle of independent power and raises it to the rank of lord falls in truth into association. And none is sincere in tawḥīd save he who sees all created things, with their attributes, as proceeding from God.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ح ب ر

versets.ar: يُرِيدُونَ أَن يُطْفِئُوا نُورَ اللَّهِ بِأَفْوَاهِهِمْ وَيَأْبَى اللَّهُ إِلَّا أَن يُتِمَّ نُورَهُ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْكَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: yurīdūna an yuṭfiʾū nūra llāhi bi-afwāhihim wa-yaʾbā llāhu illā an yutimma nūrahu wa-law kariha l-kāfirūn
versets.trad: They wish to extinguish God's light with their mouths, but God refuses save to perfect His light, though the unbelievers be loath.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who tries to veil the rays of the sun with the smoke of his torch, or to halt the decree of heaven by his ruse, or to bring down the stars with the arrows of his bow — has only shown his vanity, without reaching his aim. So too is he who imagines the path of tawḥīd could be obscured by the vapours of doubt.

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي أَرْسَلَ رَسُولَهُ بِالْهُدَىٰ وَدِينِ الْحَقِّ لِيُظْهِرَهُ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī arsala rasūlahu bi-l-hudā wa-dīni l-ḥaqqi li-yuẓhirahu ʿalā d-dīni kullihi wa-law kariha l-mushrikūn
versets.trad: It is He who sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth, to cause it to prevail over every religion, though the idolaters be loath.
themes: guidance and the religion of truth
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He removed obstacles by the proofs He made shine, and dispelled doubts by the way He made clear; the suns of truth are at their zenith, the signs of the Law luminous. "It is the sun — save that the sun sets, while the One we speak of never absents Himself."

mots.racine: د ي ن / ح ق ق

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ الْأَحْبَارِ وَالرُّهْبَانِ لَيَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوَالَ النَّاسِ بِالْبَاطِلِ وَيَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَالَّذِينَ يَكْنِزُونَ الذَّهَبَ وَالْفِضَّةَ وَلَا يُنفِقُونَهَا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَبَشِّرْهُم بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū inna kathīran mina l-aḥbāri wa-r-ruhbāni la-yaʾkulūna amwāla n-nāsi bi-l-bāṭili wa-yaṣuddūna ʿan sabīli llāh, wa-lladhīna yaknizūna dh-dhahaba wa-l-fiḍḍata wa-lā yunfiqūnahā fī sabīli llāhi fa-bashshirhum bi-ʿadhābin alīm
versets.trad: O you who believe, many of the rabbis and monks devour the goods of men wrongfully and turn them from the way of God. To those who hoard gold and silver without spending them in God's path, announce a painful chastisement,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the learned man feeds on the wealth of men in return for what he teaches, the blessings of his knowledge depart. When the gnostic profits from the service of the disciple, the traces of his aspiration fade — and his state no longer holds within the rule of tawḥīd.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ب ط ل

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يُحْمَىٰ عَلَيْهَا فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ فَتُكْوَىٰ بِهَا جِبَاهُهُمْ وَجُنُوبُهُمْ وَظُهُورُهُمْ ۖ هَٰذَا مَا كَنَزْتُمْ لِأَنفُسِكُمْ فَذُوقُوا مَا كُنتُمْ تَكْنِزُونَ
versets.tr: yawma yuḥmā ʿalayhā fī nāri jahannama fa-tukwā bihā jibāhuhum wa-junūbuhum wa-ẓuhūruhum, hādhā mā kanaztum li-anfusikum fa-dhūqū mā kuntum taknizūn
versets.trad: on the day when those treasures are heated in the fire of Gehenna, and their foreheads, sides, and backs are branded with them: "This is what you hoarded for yourselves; taste then what you used to hoard!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because they sought glory among men by their gold and withheld God's due from it, their faces were disfigured; because they leaned their backs upon their wealth, their backs were branded; because they closed their flanks to the poor who sat near them, their flanks were marked.

mots.racine: ك و ي / ج ب ه
mots.sens: their foreheads are branded with them

versets.ar: إِنَّ عِدَّةَ الشُّهُورِ عِندَ اللَّهِ اثْنَا عَشَرَ شَهْرًا فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ يَوْمَ خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ مِنْهَا أَرْبَعَةٌ حُرُمٌ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ الدِّينُ الْقَيِّمُ ۚ فَلَا تَظْلِمُوا فِيهِنَّ أَنفُسَكُمْ ۚ وَقَاتِلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ كَافَّةً كَمَا يُقَاتِلُونَكُمْ كَافَّةً ۚ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: inna ʿiddata sh-shuhūri ʿinda llāhi thnā ʿashara shahran fī kitābi llāhi yawma khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa minhā arbaʿatun ḥurum, dhālika d-dīnu l-qayyim, fa-lā taẓlimū fīhinna anfusakum, wa-qātilū l-mushrikīna kāffatan kamā yuqātilūnakum kāffah, wa-ʿlamū anna llāha maʿa l-muttaqīn
versets.trad: The number of months with God is twelve, in God's Book on the day He created the heavens and the earth; of them four are sacred. That is the upright religion. So wrong not yourselves therein. And fight the idolaters all together, as they fight you all together, and know that God is with the godfearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens a horizon: for the elect among His servants, all the months are Shaʿbān and Ramaḍān; all days are Friday; every place is a mosque. And: injustice to the self is to surrender the reins to the passions, which lead to places of perdition. The weapon of combat? There is no sharper blade against the enemy than your disengagement from your own strength and power.

mots.racine: ع د د / ش ه ر
ر ب ع / ح ر م
mots.def: Muḥarram, Rajab, Dhū l-qaʿda, Dhū l-ḥijja.

mots.racine: د ي ن / ق و م

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا النَّسِيءُ زِيَادَةٌ فِي الْكُفْرِ يُضَلُّ بِهِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يُحِلُّونَهُ عَامًا وَيُحَرِّمُونَهُ عَامًا لِّيُوَاطِئُوا عِدَّةَ مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ فَيُحِلُّوا مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ ۚ زُيِّنَ لَهُمْ سُوءُ أَعْمَالِهِمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: innamā n-nasīʾu ziyādatun fī l-kufri yuḍallu bihi lladhīna kafarū yuḥillūnahu ʿāman wa-yuḥarrimūnahu ʿāman li-yuwāṭiʾū ʿiddata mā ḥarrama llāhu fa-yuḥillū mā ḥarrama llāh, zuyyina lahum sūʾu aʿmālihim, wa-llāhu lā yahdī l-qawma l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: The postponement [of a sacred month] is only an increase in disbelief, by which those who reject faith are led astray. One year they hold it lawful, another year forbidden, to match the count of what God has hallowed, and so they make lawful what God has forbidden. Their evil deeds are made fair to them. God does not guide a disbelieving people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: religion is to watch the command, shun the burden, never run ahead of God in any judgement of the Law. The terms of works are fixed, uprightness lies in upholding servanthood: the month is what God has named month; the year and the cycle are what He has set in His Law.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَا لَكُمْ إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمُ انفِرُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ اثَّاقَلْتُمْ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ ۚ أَرَضِيتُم بِالْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا مِنَ الْآخِرَةِ ۚ فَمَا مَتَاعُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا فِي الْآخِرَةِ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū mā lakum idhā qīla lakumu nfirū fī sabīli llāhi ththāqaltum ilā l-arḍ, a-raḍītum bi-l-ḥayāti d-dunyā mina l-ākhirah, fa-mā matāʿu l-ḥayāti d-dunyā fī l-ākhirati illā qalīl
versets.trad: O you who believe, what is the matter with you that, when it is said "march forth in the way of God," you cling heavily to the earth? Are you so content with the life of this world over the next? Yet the enjoyment of this life is, in the next, but little.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī rebukes inertia as symptom: the leaning toward laziness, the rest in heaviness, are signs of weak faith — for faith is an exacting creditor, accepting from the servant only the practice of the harder way and attachment to the truer good. And: does it befit the adorer to choose his world over his hereafter? Does it befit the gnostic to prefer his passion to the pleasure of his Master?

mots.def: nafara: to set out on campaign.

versets.ar: إِلَّا تَنفِرُوا يُعَذِّبْكُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا وَيَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْمًا غَيْرَكُمْ وَلَا تَضُرُّوهُ شَيْئًا ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: illā tanfirū yuʿadhdhibkum ʿadhāban alīman wa-yastabdil qawman ghayrakum wa-lā taḍurrūhu shayʾan, wa-llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: If you do not march forth, He will inflict a painful chastisement upon you and replace you with another people. You will not harm Him in the least. God is Powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī probes the "painful chastisement": it is, when the servant turns away, that no army of tawfīq be sent after him to bring him back to the threshold. It is that he be deprived of the sweetness of intimate converse when he returns. It is being shut out on the day of meeting — rejection, whose mere promise is harder than separation itself.

mots.sens: if you do not march forth

versets.ar: إِلَّا تَنصُرُوهُ فَقَدْ نَصَرَهُ اللَّهُ إِذْ أَخْرَجَهُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ثَانِيَ اثْنَيْنِ إِذْ هُمَا فِي الْغَارِ إِذْ يَقُولُ لِصَاحِبِهِ لَا تَحْزَنْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَنَا ۖ فَأَنزَلَ اللَّهُ سَكِينَتَهُ عَلَيْهِ وَأَيَّدَهُ بِجُنُودٍ لَّمْ تَرَوْهَا وَجَعَلَ كَلِمَةَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا السُّفْلَىٰ ۗ وَكَلِمَةُ اللَّهِ هِيَ الْعُلْيَا ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: illā tanṣurūhu fa-qad naṣarahu llāhu idh akhrajahu lladhīna kafarū thāniya thnayni idh humā fī l-ghāri idh yaqūlu li-ṣāḥibihi lā taḥzan inna llāha maʿanā, fa-anzala llāhu sakīnatahu ʿalayhi wa-ayyadahu bi-junūdin lam tarawhā wa-jaʿala kalimata lladhīna kafarū s-suflā, wa-kalimatu llāhi hiya l-ʿulyā, wa-llāhu ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: If you do not help him, God has already helped him, when the unbelievers drove him out, the second of two, when they were in the cave, when he said to his companion: "Grieve not; God is with us." Then God sent down His Sakīna upon him, supported him with armies you did not see, and made the word of those who disbelieved lowest, while the Word of God is highest. God is Mighty, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets several pearls. First: "Are they not two, of whom God is the third?" — such is the nobility of the support. Then: "O seeker of God in the lofty Throne, seek not the Throne: glory is in the cave." On the Sakīna: it descends upon the Ṣiddīq as a singular grace, as it is said: "God manifests to people in general, and to Abū Bakr in particular." And the Ṣiddīq's grief, he says, was for the Prophet alone, not for himself.

mots.racine: ك ل م / ع ل و
mots.sens: the Word of God is the highest

versets.ar: انفِرُوا خِفَافًا وَثِقَالًا وَجَاهِدُوا بِأَمْوَالِكُمْ وَأَنفُسِكُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: infirū khifāfan wa-thiqālan wa-jāhidū bi-amwālikum wa-anfusikum fī sabīli llāh, dhālikum khayrun lakum in kuntum taʿlamūn
versets.trad: March forth, light or heavy, and strive with your wealth and your persons in the way of God: that is better for you, if only you knew.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "light" — when your hearts are present and no fatigue of striving touches you; "heavy" — when you are returned to yourselves in the burden of trials. Or: "light" when freed from the bondage of demands and choice; "heavy" when upon your hearts lies the weight of needs, while you hope from the Real the granting of your aims.

mots.racine: خ ف ف / ث ق ل

versets.ar: لَوْ كَانَ عَرَضًا قَرِيبًا وَسَفَرًا قَاصِدًا لَّاتَّبَعُوكَ وَلَٰكِن بَعُدَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الشُّقَّةُ ۚ وَسَيَحْلِفُونَ بِاللَّهِ لَوِ اسْتَطَعْنَا لَخَرَجْنَا مَعَكُمْ يُهْلِكُونَ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ إِنَّهُمْ لَكَاذِبُونَ
versets.tr: law kāna ʿaraḍan qarīban wa-safaran qāṣidan la-ttabaʿūka wa-lākin baʿudat ʿalayhimu sh-shuqqah, wa-sa-yaḥlifūna bi-llāhi lawi staṭaʿnā la-kharajnā maʿakum yuhlikūna anfusahum wa-llāhu yaʿlamu innahum la-kādhibūn
versets.trad: Had it been a near gain or an easy journey, they would have followed you, but the distance seemed too long. They will swear by God: "Had we been able, we would have gone out with you." They destroy themselves, and God knows they are liars.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he whose aim is not true, his effort does not reach its end — he lives on an edge, behaves by fits and starts. The oath of the temporizer is a lying oath; the eyes of perspicacity bear witness against it, and hearts recoil from it.

mots.racine: ع ر ض / ق ر ب
ه ل ك / ن ف س

versets.ar: عَفَا اللَّهُ عَنكَ لِمَ أَذِنتَ لَهُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَكَ الَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا وَتَعْلَمَ الْكَاذِبِينَ
versets.tr: ʿafā llāhu ʿanka lima adhinta lahum ḥattā yatabayyana laka lladhīna ṣadaqū wa-taʿlama l-kādhibīn
versets.trad: May God pardon you! Why did you give them leave before it became plain to you who were truthful, and you knew the liars?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the delicacy: God places the pardon before the appearance of reproach — such is the custom of friends with friends. And he adds: the good deeds of enemies, though good, are as if rejected; the faults of loved ones, though faults, are as if forgiven: "Who would blame the one he loves for his fault, while an intercessor in the heart pleads for him?"

mots.def: ʿafā: to wipe away the fault.

versets.ar: لَا يَسْتَأْذِنُكَ الَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ أَن يُجَاهِدُوا بِأَمْوَالِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِالْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: lā yastaʾdhinuka lladhīna yuʾminūna bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri an yujāhidū bi-amwālihim wa-anfusihim, wa-llāhu ʿalīmun bi-l-muttaqīn
versets.trad: Those who believe in God and the Last Day do not ask your leave to be excused from striving with their wealth and their persons. God knows the godfearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sincere in his conviction prefers nothing to his command, withholds nothing he can use, in exhausting his strength, spending his effort, enduring his hardship, exercising his ardour.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا يَسْتَأْذِنُكَ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَارْتَابَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ فَهُمْ فِي رَيْبِهِمْ يَتَرَدَّدُونَ
versets.tr: innamā yastaʾdhinuka lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-rtābat qulūbuhum fa-hum fī raybihim yataraddadūn
versets.trad: Only those who do not believe in God and the Last Day ask your leave, those whose hearts have doubted; in their doubt they waver.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever seeks to escape the engagement seizes upon delay and withdrawal as a chance — through lack of faith, through the rooting of doubt in his heart and secret. These are the ones who turn in their doubt, who hesitate in their scepticism.

mots.def: irtāba: to be seized by doubt.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَرَادُوا الْخُرُوجَ لَأَعَدُّوا لَهُ عُدَّةً وَلَٰكِن كَرِهَ اللَّهُ انبِعَاثَهُمْ فَثَبَّطَهُمْ وَقِيلَ اقْعُدُوا مَعَ الْقَاعِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-law arādū l-khurūja la-aʿaddū lahu ʿuddatan wa-lākin kariha llāhu nbiʿāthahum fa-thabbaṭahum wa-qīla qʿudū maʿa l-qāʿidīn
versets.trad: Had they intended to go forth, they would have prepared for it; but God disliked their setting out, so He held them back, and it was said: "Sit with those who sit."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: had they been truthful in obedience, they would have answered by spending their power and energy; but their will sickened, and their stupor held them short of departure. He compelled them by duty, and fixed them at home by withdrawal: the call by obligation, the dismissal by what He actually does.

versets.ar: لَوْ خَرَجُوا فِيكُم مَّا زَادُوكُمْ إِلَّا خَبَالًا وَلَأَوْضَعُوا خِلَالَكُمْ يَبْغُونَكُمُ الْفِتْنَةَ وَفِيكُمْ سَمَّاعُونَ لَهُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِالظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: law kharajū fīkum mā zādūkum illā khabālan wa-la-awḍaʿū khilālakum yabghūnakumu l-fitnata wa-fīkum sammāʿūna lahum, wa-llāhu ʿalīmun bi-ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: Had they gone out with you, they would have only added confusion and stirred up sedition among you, for some of you would have listened eagerly to them. God knows the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he whose harm exceeds his benefit, his absence profits more than his presence; he from whom nothing comes but ill, his withdrawal is more useful than his coming.

versets.ar: لَقَدِ ابْتَغَوُا الْفِتْنَةَ مِن قَبْلُ وَقَلَّبُوا لَكَ الْأُمُورَ حَتَّىٰ جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَظَهَرَ أَمْرُ اللَّهِ وَهُمْ كَارِهُونَ
versets.tr: laqadi btaghawu l-fitnata min qablu wa-qallabū laka l-umūra ḥattā jāʾa l-ḥaqqu wa-ẓahara amru llāhi wa-hum kārihūn
versets.trad: They had already sought sedition before, and turned matters over against you, until the truth came and God's command appeared, against their wishes.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they openly displayed concord while hatching hypocrisy; they professed support and secretly aimed to confound your affairs. But God laid bare their disgraces and exposed them, until, knowing their secrets, you took your guard against them.

versets.ar: وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَقُولُ ائْذَن لِّي وَلَا تَفْتِنِّي ۚ أَلَا فِي الْفِتْنَةِ سَقَطُوا ۗ وَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ لَمُحِيطَةٌ بِالْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-minhum man yaqūlu iʾdhan lī wa-lā taftinnī, a-lā fī l-fitnati saqaṭū, wa-inna jahannama la-muḥīṭatun bi-l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: Among them is he who says: "Excuse me, and do not put me to the test!" Yet into the test they have already fallen, and Gehenna shall enclose the unbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they paraded their vile act in the garb of an excuse; they wished to disguise from the Messenger and the believers the foulness of their behaviour and their secret. But God showed that into what they pretended to fear, they had already fallen by their very deed.

versets.ar: إِن تُصِبْكَ حَسَنَةٌ تَسُؤْهُمْ ۖ وَإِن تُصِبْكَ مُصِيبَةٌ يَقُولُوا قَدْ أَخَذْنَا أَمْرَنَا مِن قَبْلُ وَيَتَوَلَّوْا وَّهُمْ فَرِحُونَ
versets.tr: in tuṣibka ḥasanatun tasuʾhum, wa-in tuṣibka muṣībatun yaqūlū qad akhadhnā amranā min qablu wa-yatawallaw wa-hum fariḥūn
versets.trad: If good befalls you, it grieves them; if calamity strikes you, they say: "We had taken our precautions," and turn away rejoicing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: such is the envier: the moan of his heart rises at the sight of another's grace; nothing gladdens his heart but the falling of trial upon another. There is no cure for the envier's wounds save the loss of the grace. God hastens the envier's punishment: the pain of his heart at the well-being of his envied — grace for the envied, paid in advance; anguish for the envier, paid in advance too.

versets.ar: قُل لَّن يُصِيبَنَا إِلَّا مَا كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَنَا هُوَ مَوْلَانَا ۚ وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: qul lan yuṣībanā illā mā kataba llāhu lanā huwa mawlānā, wa-ʿalā llāhi fa-l-yatawakkali l-muʾminūn
versets.trad: Say: "Nothing shall befall us save what God has written for us. He is our Protector. In God let the believers put their trust."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the believer is untouched by the malign joy of his enemy, for he sees nothing but the Friend's will; he knows that what touches him is his Master's will, and meets its harshness in the breath of His good pleasure: what seemed hard becomes sweet. And: the beginning of tawakkul is trust in His promise; then contentment with His choice; then the forgetting of your own affairs by the overflow of His remembrance in your heart.

versets.ar: قُلْ هَلْ تَرَبَّصُونَ بِنَا إِلَّا إِحْدَى الْحُسْنَيَيْنِ ۖ وَنَحْنُ نَتَرَبَّصُ بِكُمْ أَن يُصِيبَكُمُ اللَّهُ بِعَذَابٍ مِّنْ عِندِهِ أَوْ بِأَيْدِينَا ۖ فَتَرَبَّصُوا إِنَّا مَعَكُم مُّتَرَبِّصُونَ
versets.tr: qul hal tarabbaṣūna binā illā iḥdā l-ḥusnayayni wa-naḥnu natarabbaṣu bikum an yuṣībakumu llāhu bi-ʿadhābin min ʿindihi aw bi-aydīnā, fa-tarabbaṣū innā maʿakum mutarabbiṣūn
versets.trad: Say: "Do you await for us anything but one of two finest outcomes? While for you, we await that God will smite you with a chastisement from Himself or by our hands. So wait — we too are waiting with you."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the two fairest are either the fulfilment of God's right in the present — and this is, in truth, the greater Garden; or the arrival to God at the end through the seal of martyrdom, and the nearness in the hereafter — and this is the highest dignity.

mots.racine: و ح د / ح س ن

versets.ar: قُلْ أَنفِقُوا طَوْعًا أَوْ كَرْهًا لَّن يُتَقَبَّلَ مِنكُمْ ۖ إِنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ قَوْمًا فَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: qul anfiqū ṭawʿan aw karhan lan yutaqabbala minkum, innakum kuntum qawman fāsiqīn
versets.trad: Say: "Spend, willingly or unwillingly — it will never be accepted from you. You are a corrupt people."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the rejected one is not received by his supplication; the decree of his misery is not changed by piling up feigned efforts. The enemy's nearness only breeds aversion; the lover's tenderness only breeds increased affection.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ك ر ه

versets.ar: وَمَا مَنَعَهُمْ أَن تُقْبَلَ مِنْهُمْ نَفَقَاتُهُمْ إِلَّا أَنَّهُمْ كَفَرُوا بِاللَّهِ وَبِرَسُولِهِ وَلَا يَأْتُونَ الصَّلَاةَ إِلَّا وَهُمْ كُسَالَىٰ وَلَا يُنفِقُونَ إِلَّا وَهُمْ كَارِهُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā manaʿahum an tuqbala minhum nafaqātuhum illā annahum kafarū bi-llāhi wa-bi-rasūlihi wa-lā yaʾtūna ṣ-ṣalāta illā wa-hum kusālā wa-lā yunfiqūna illā wa-hum kārihūn
versets.trad: Nothing prevents the acceptance of their spendings except that they have disbelieved in God and His Messenger, that they come to prayer only sluggishly, and spend only with reluctance.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they lost sincerity in their wealth, so they were deprived of election in their states; they were denied salvation in this world and the next. He who obeys by mere habit — without an ardour of will driving him to it — finds in his obedience no repose nor growth.

versets.ar: فَلَا تُعْجِبْكَ أَمْوَالُهُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُهُمْ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ لِيُعَذِّبَهُم بِهَا فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَتَزْهَقَ أَنفُسُهُمْ وَهُمْ كَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lā tuʿjibka amwāluhum wa-lā awlāduhum, innamā yurīdu llāhu li-yuʿadhdhibahum bihā fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-tazhaqa anfusuhum wa-hum kāfirūn
versets.trad: Let not their wealth or children dazzle you. God only wills to chastise them through these in the life of this world, and that their souls depart while they are unbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what they took for grace, what they counted as favour from God, is in truth a trial, a cause of misery and severance: the decree slipped poison of bitter aloes into the drink they savoured.

mots.racine: ز ه ق / ن ف س

versets.ar: وَيَحْلِفُونَ بِاللَّهِ إِنَّهُمْ لَمِنكُمْ وَمَا هُم مِّنكُمْ وَلَٰكِنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ يَفْرَقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yaḥlifūna bi-llāhi innahum la-minkum wa-mā hum minkum wa-lākinnahum qawmun yafraqūn
versets.trad: They swear by God that they are of you, though they are not of you; they are a people in dread.
themes: the oath that fails to convince
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the quest for nearness through perjured oaths breeds in hearts only distance from acceptance. False adornment does not soothe the secrets, does not heal the inner sight by trust. What is not to be has no place by trickery; what is to be will be.

versets.ar: لَوْ يَجِدُونَ مَلْجَأً أَوْ مَغَارَاتٍ أَوْ مُدَّخَلًا لَّوَلَّوْا إِلَيْهِ وَهُمْ يَجْمَحُونَ
versets.tr: law yajidūna maljaʾan aw maghārātin aw muddakhalan la-wallaw ilayhi wa-hum yajmaḥūn
versets.trad: Could they find a refuge, caves, or any hiding place, they would turn to it and rush headlong.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the temporizer in friendship slips out of its thread by the slightest pretext; if he finds an outlet, he takes refuge in it, hoping for a chance to slip away.

versets.ar: وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَلْمِزُكَ فِي الصَّدَقَاتِ فَإِنْ أُعْطُوا مِنْهَا رَضُوا وَإِن لَّمْ يُعْطَوْا مِنْهَا إِذَا هُمْ يَسْخَطُونَ
versets.tr: wa-minhum man yalmizuka fī ṣ-ṣadaqāti fa-in uʿṭū minhā raḍū wa-in lam yuʿṭaw minhā idhā hum yaskhaṭūn
versets.trad: Among them is he who finds fault with you concerning the alms: if given a share they are pleased, if not, they are furious.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they are the people of greed: they flatter on the surface so long as the gains keep coming; once cut off, they turn as if there had never been affection. Whoever has his satisfaction in cause and his resentment in lack belongs not to the people of true loyalty.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ رَضُوا مَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَقَالُوا حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ سَيُؤْتِينَا اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ وَرَسُولُهُ إِنَّا إِلَى اللَّهِ رَاغِبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law annahum raḍū mā ātāhumu llāhu wa-rasūluhu wa-qālū ḥasbunā llāhu sa-yuʾtīnā llāhu min faḍlihi wa-rasūluhu innā ilā llāhi rāghibūn
versets.trad: If only they were content with what God and His Messenger gave them, and said: "God is enough for us; God will give us of His bounty, and so will His Messenger. To God we aspire!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: had they kept themselves with God in the secret of contentment, the varieties of gift and the realizations of what is longed for would have come to them; they would have kept, at the finding, the courtesy due to God, without weariness or pain. But they halted in the homelands of greed and fell into humiliation and combat.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا الصَّدَقَاتُ لِلْفُقَرَاءِ وَالْمَسَاكِينِ وَالْعَامِلِينَ عَلَيْهَا وَالْمُؤَلَّفَةِ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَفِي الرِّقَابِ وَالْغَارِمِينَ وَفِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ ۖ فَرِيضَةً مِّنَ اللَّهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: innamā ṣ-ṣadaqātu li-l-fuqarāʾi wa-l-masākīni wa-l-ʿāmilīna ʿalayhā wa-l-muʾallafati qulūbuhum wa-fī r-riqābi wa-l-ghārimīna wa-fī sabīli llāhi wa-bni s-sabīli, farīḍatan mina llāh, wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīm
versets.trad: The alms are only for the poor and the destitute, those who collect them, those whose hearts are to be reconciled, for the freeing of slaves and the debt-burdened, for God's path and the wayfarer — a duty enjoined by God. God is Knowing, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī here distinguishes inner degrees: need (ḥāja), poverty (faqr), destitution (maskana). The needy is content with this world, and this world fills his lack; the poor suffices himself with the hereafter, and the Garden mends his indigence; but the miskīn is content with his Master alone — he turns neither to this world, nor to the hereafter, nor to any other than Him. Hence the Prophet's prayer: "Let me live miskīn, let me die miskīn, raise me with the masākīn."

mots.racine: أ ل ف / ق ل ب
mots.sens: those whose hearts are to be reconciled
mots.def: converts or sympathizers to bind closer.

versets.ar: وَمِنْهُمُ الَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ النَّبِيَّ وَيَقُولُونَ هُوَ أُذُنٌ ۚ قُلْ أُذُنُ خَيْرٍ لَّكُمْ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَيُؤْمِنُ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنكُمْ ۚ وَالَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-minhumu lladhīna yuʾdhūna n-nabiyya wa-yaqūlūna huwa udhun, qul udhunu khayrin lakum yuʾminu bi-llāhi wa-yuʾminu li-l-muʾminīna wa-raḥmatun li-lladhīna āmanū minkum, wa-lladhīna yuʾdhūna rasūla llāhi lahum ʿadhābun alīm
versets.trad: Among them are those who offend the Prophet, saying: "He is all ears." Say: "An ear of good for you. He believes in God, trusts the believers, and is a mercy to those of you who believe." Those who offend God's Messenger, for them a painful chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they blamed him for what is precisely the mark of his nobility and grace: he listens, out of beauty of character, to what is said to him. As the Prophet affirmed: "The believer is unsuspecting and noble; the hypocrite, crafty and base." Who is the wise? — the discerning who feigns inattention.

mots.def: figure: he who lends ear to all.

versets.ar: يَحْلِفُونَ بِاللَّهِ لَكُمْ لِيُرْضُوكُمْ وَاللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَحَقُّ أَن يُرْضُوهُ إِن كَانُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: yaḥlifūna bi-llāhi lakum li-yurḍūkum wa-llāhu wa-rasūluhu aḥaqqu an yurḍūhu in kānū muʾminīn
versets.trad: They swear by God to you to please you, but God and His Messenger have more right to be pleased, if they were believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who adorns himself for the creatures, seeks their nearness and their constant satisfaction, and follows their passion in this — God strips him of his rank in their eyes. The loser is he who leaves what he would be thanked for and prefers what brings him no reward.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوا أَنَّهُ مَن يُحَادِدِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ فَأَنَّ لَهُ نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ خَالِدًا فِيهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ الْخِزْيُ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: a-lam yaʿlamū annahu man yuḥādidi llāha wa-rasūlahu fa-anna lahu nāra jahannama khālidan fīhā, dhālika l-khizyu l-ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: Do they not know that whoever opposes God and His Messenger, for him is the Fire of Gehenna, to abide therein? That is the immense disgrace.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who has denied God and associated in His Unicity by setting up an imagined being has earned what is God's due: hastened punishment in the present by separation, and pernicious endurance in the term by the everlasting fire.

mots.def: ḥādda: to stand on the opposite border.

versets.ar: يَحْذَرُ الْمُنَافِقُونَ أَن تُنَزَّلَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُورَةٌ تُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ ۚ قُلِ اسْتَهْزِئُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ مُخْرِجٌ مَّا تَحْذَرُونَ
versets.tr: yaḥdharu l-munāfiqūna an tunazzala ʿalayhim sūratun tunabbiʾuhum bi-mā fī qulūbihim, quli stahziʾū inna llāha mukhrijun mā taḥdharūn
versets.trad: The hypocrites dread that a sūra should be sent down upon them, telling them what is in their hearts. Say: "Mock on! God will bring out what you dread."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they thought the Real would not expose them, claimed innocence, denied what their secrets concealed. Then God loosened the rein of their respite, then tore the veil from their hypocrisy: He laid them bare before the people of verification, and they had to veil themselves in the scarf of shame.

versets.ar: وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُمْ لَيَقُولُنَّ إِنَّمَا كُنَّا نَخُوضُ وَنَلْعَبُ ۚ قُلْ أَبِاللَّهِ وَآيَاتِهِ وَرَسُولِهِ كُنتُمْ تَسْتَهْزِئُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-in saʾaltahum la-yaqūlunna innamā kunnā nakhūḍu wa-nalʿabu, qul a-bi-llāhi wa-āyātihi wa-rasūlihi kuntum tastahziʾūn
versets.trad: If you ask them, they will say: "We were only chatting and playing." Say: "Was it God, His signs, and His Messenger you were mocking?"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who treats the religion lightly, without fear of breaking the sanctity of Islam, God makes of him on the spot a warning sign and humbles him in the hereafter. The Real is not content until He makes the insolent taste His might.

versets.ar: لَا تَعْتَذِرُوا قَدْ كَفَرْتُم بَعْدَ إِيمَانِكُمْ ۚ إِن نَّعْفُ عَن طَائِفَةٍ مِّنكُمْ نُعَذِّبْ طَائِفَةً بِأَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا مُجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: lā taʿtadhirū qad kafartum baʿda īmānikum, in naʿfu ʿan ṭāʾifatin minkum nuʿadhdhib ṭāʾifatan bi-annahum kānū mujrimīn
versets.trad: Make no excuse! You have disbelieved after your faith. Even if We pardon a group of you, We shall chastise another, for they were sinners.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws the doctrinal point: God detaches pardon and chastisement from any cause binding upon the creature; He refers them to pure will. For if the servant's attributes compelled them, with equal attributes the decision would be equal; yet He pardons some and chastises others in one same state — proof that He does what He wills, singles out whom He wills by what He wills.

versets.ar: الْمُنَافِقُونَ وَالْمُنَافِقَاتُ بَعْضُهُم مِّن بَعْضٍ ۚ يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمُنكَرِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَقْبِضُونَ أَيْدِيَهُمْ ۚ نَسُوا اللَّهَ فَنَسِيَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: al-munāfiqūna wa-l-munāfiqātu baʿḍuhum min baʿḍ, yaʾmurūna bi-l-munkari wa-yanhawna ʿani l-maʿrūfi wa-yaqbiḍūna aydiyahum, nasū llāha fa-nasiyahum, inna l-munāfiqīna humu l-fāsiqūn
versets.trad: The hypocrites, men and women, are of one another. They enjoin wrong, forbid right, and clench their hands. They forgot God, so He forgot them. The hypocrites — they are the corrupt.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the believer is strengthened by the believer; the hypocrite is propped up by the hypocrite; birds of the sky settle with their like. The hypocrite strengthens in his companion what ruins him; the believer enlightens the believer about his faults and makes his sins hateful to him. On God's forgetting: He repaid them for their forgetting by calling the recompense "forgetting" — He left them to what they chose for themselves.

mots.sens: they forgot God, so He forgot them

versets.ar: وَعَدَ اللَّهُ الْمُنَافِقِينَ وَالْمُنَافِقَاتِ وَالْكُفَّارَ نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ هِيَ حَسْبُهُمْ ۚ وَلَعَنَهُمُ اللَّهُ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّقِيمٌ
versets.tr: waʿada llāhu l-munāfiqīna wa-l-munāfiqāti wa-l-kuffāra nāra jahannama khālidīna fīhā, hiya ḥasbuhum, wa-laʿanahumu llāh, wa-lahum ʿadhābun muqīm
versets.trad: God has promised the hypocrites, men and women, and the unbelievers, the Fire of Gehenna, where they shall abide. That is enough for them. God has cursed them, and theirs is a lasting chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He promised them the Fire in the hereafter; already, in the present, lasting chastisement is theirs — their deferred punishment is the burning, their present punishment is the severance.

versets.ar: كَالَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ كَانُوا أَشَدَّ مِنكُمْ قُوَّةً وَأَكْثَرَ أَمْوَالًا وَأَوْلَادًا فَاسْتَمْتَعُوا بِخَلَاقِهِمْ فَاسْتَمْتَعْتُم بِخَلَاقِكُمْ كَمَا اسْتَمْتَعَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُم بِخَلَاقِهِمْ وَخُضْتُمْ كَالَّذِي خَاضُوا ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَالُهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: ka-lladhīna min qablikum kānū ashadda minkum quwwatan wa-akthara amwālan wa-awlādan fa-stamtaʿū bi-khalāqihim fa-stamtaʿtum bi-khalāqikum kamā stamtaʿa lladhīna min qablikum bi-khalāqihim wa-khuḍtum ka-lladhī khāḍū, ūlāʾika ḥabiṭat aʿmāluhum fī d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirah, wa-ūlāʾika humu l-khāsirūn
versets.trad: You act like those before you, who were stronger than you, richer in wealth and children. They enjoyed their portion, and you have enjoyed yours as those before you enjoyed theirs; you have plunged in talk as they did. Their works fell void in this world and the next: those are the losers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: you have followed the way of those who preceded you in disbelief and hypocrisy, and We have dealt with them as they deserved. They had upon you the advantage of strength and number, and We dealt with them by Our wont with the people of division and duplicity; their peace did not last, their armament served them not on the day of trial.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ يَأْتِهِمْ نَبَأُ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ وَعَادٍ وَثَمُودَ وَقَوْمِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَأَصْحَابِ مَدْيَنَ وَالْمُؤْتَفِكَاتِ ۚ أَتَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ ۖ فَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيَظْلِمَهُمْ وَلَٰكِن كَانُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: a-lam yaʾtihim nabaʾu lladhīna min qablihim qawmi nūḥin wa-ʿādin wa-thamūda wa-qawmi ibrāhīma wa-aṣḥābi madyana wa-l-muʾtafikāt, atathum rusuluhum bi-l-bayyināt, fa-mā kāna llāhu li-yaẓlimahum wa-lākin kānū anfusahum yaẓlimūn
versets.trad: Has there not reached them the tidings of those before them — the people of Noah, of ʿĀd and Thamūd, of Abraham, of Madyan, and of the overthrown cities? Their messengers brought them clear signs. God did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the tale of the past generations and the tidings of the vanished peoples: how We destroyed their gathering, how We scattered their union. We decreed against them in justice, judged the extirpation of all; nothing of them remained, neither a stoker of fire, nor any but shame and dishonour.

versets.ar: وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتُ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ ۚ يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ وَيُقِيمُونَ الصَّلَاةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ الزَّكَاةَ وَيُطِيعُونَ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ سَيَرْحَمُهُمُ اللَّهُ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-l-muʾminūna wa-l-muʾminātu baʿḍuhum awliyāʾu baʿḍ, yaʾmurūna bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-yanhawna ʿani l-munkari wa-yuqīmūna ṣ-ṣalāta wa-yuʾtūna z-zakāta wa-yuṭīʿūna llāha wa-rasūlah, ūlāʾika sa-yarḥamuhumu llāh, inna llāha ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: The believing men and women are intimates of one another: they enjoin right and forbid wrong, perform the prayer, give the alms, and obey God and His Messenger. Upon these God will have mercy: truly God is Mighty, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they help one another in obedience, recommend to each other the abandonment of what is forbidden; they love one another in God, fulfil God's right, keep company for God, and oppose one another for God alone. They have left their shares for God's right, preferred God's pleasure to their passion. These God has safeguarded in the present, and will have mercy upon at the term.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و

versets.ar: وَعَدَ اللَّهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا وَمَسَاكِنَ طَيِّبَةً فِي جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ ۚ وَرِضْوَانٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: waʿada llāhu l-muʾminīna wa-l-muʾmināti jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā wa-masākina ṭayyibatan fī jannāti ʿadnin, wa-riḍwānun mina llāhi akbar, dhālika huwa l-fawzu l-ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: God has promised the believing men and women Gardens with streams running beneath, where they shall abide, and goodly dwellings in the Gardens of Eden. But a good pleasure from God is greater: that is the supreme triumph.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the dwelling is good only at the sight of the Beloved. "And a good pleasure from God, greater" — the mark of the people of Riḍwān is that they find its taste: they are in the breath of intimacy (rūḥ al-uns), and that breath does not pale before the rest of the holy abode; it is more complete and more grandiose.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ع د ن
ف و ز / ع ظ م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ جَاهِدِ الْكُفَّارَ وَالْمُنَافِقِينَ وَاغْلُظْ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَمَأْوَاهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu jāhidi l-kuffāra wa-l-munāfiqīna wa-ghluẓ ʿalayhim, wa-maʾwāhum jahannamu, wa-biʾsa l-maṣīr
versets.trad: O Prophet, strive against the unbelievers and the hypocrites, and be harsh with them. Their abode is Gehenna: a wretched end!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to Moses He had said: "Speak gently to him"; to our Prophet: "Be stern with them." But this, after the showing of proofs and the removal of every excuse by respite. The struggle begins with the tongue, the unfolding of the argument; then, if the enemy denies after the removal of excuse, by warning; then, if speech does not avail, by combat and the spending of full effort in the struggle.

versets.ar: يَحْلِفُونَ بِاللَّهِ مَا قَالُوا وَلَقَدْ قَالُوا كَلِمَةَ الْكُفْرِ وَكَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِسْلَامِهِمْ وَهَمُّوا بِمَا لَمْ يَنَالُوا ۚ وَمَا نَقَمُوا إِلَّا أَنْ أَغْنَاهُمُ اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ ۚ فَإِن يَتُوبُوا يَكُ خَيْرًا لَّهُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَتَوَلَّوْا يُعَذِّبْهُمُ اللَّهُ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ۚ وَمَا لَهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مِن وَلِيٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ
versets.tr: yaḥlifūna bi-llāhi mā qālū wa-laqad qālū kalimata l-kufri wa-kafarū baʿda islāmihim wa-hammū bi-mā lam yanālū, wa-mā naqamū illā an aghnāhumu llāhu wa-rasūluhu min faḍlih, fa-in yatūbū yaku khayran lahum, wa-in yatawallaw yuʿadhdhibhumu llāhu ʿadhāban alīman fī d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirah, wa-mā lahum fī l-arḍi min waliyyin wa-lā naṣīr
versets.trad: They swear by God that they did not say it, yet they did utter the word of disbelief and disbelieved after their submission; they aimed at what they did not attain. They resented only that God and His Messenger had enriched them by His bounty. If they repent, it shall be better for them; but if they turn away, God will chastise them with a painful chastisement in this world and the next, and they shall have on earth no protector nor helper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they hid behind their oaths, but God tore their veils and laid bare their secrets. The strongest pillar of repentance is to untie the knot of persistence in the fault, in the heart; then the fulfilment of every right of the command, to the utmost.

mots.racine: ك ل م / ك ف ر

versets.ar: وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ عَاهَدَ اللَّهَ لَئِنْ آتَانَا مِن فَضْلِهِ لَنَصَّدَّقَنَّ وَلَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-minhum man ʿāhada llāha la-in ātānā min faḍlihi la-naṣṣaddaqanna wa-la-nakūnanna mina ṣ-ṣāliḥīn
versets.trad: Among them is he who covenanted with God: "If He grants us of His bounty, we shall give alms and be among the righteous."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: among them are those who reinforced their covenant with God, then broke it; the misfortune of that act struck them, so that they remained for ever in their hypocrisy.

mots.def: ʿāhada: to bind by solemn pact.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا آتَاهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِ بَخِلُوا بِهِ وَتَوَلَّوا وَّهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā ātāhum min faḍlihi bakhilū bihi wa-tawallaw wa-hum muʿriḍūn
versets.trad: But when He granted them of His bounty, they were miserly with it and turned away in aversion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he sought his Lord's kindness, drew near by tightening his pact; when God realised his request, he tore what he had bound, stripped himself of what he had imposed. Avarice seized him, he withheld God's right — and the bad fortune of his hypocrisy held him captive forever.

mots.def: bakhila: to withhold what is due.

versets.ar: فَأَعْقَبَهُمْ نِفَاقًا فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ يَلْقَوْنَهُ بِمَا أَخْلَفُوا اللَّهَ مَا وَعَدُوهُ وَبِمَا كَانُوا يَكْذِبُونَ
versets.tr: fa-aʿqabahum nifāqan fī qulūbihim ilā yawmi yalqawnahu bi-mā akhlafū llāha mā waʿadūhu wa-bi-mā kānū yakdhibūn
versets.trad: So He caused hypocrisy to follow in their hearts until the day they shall meet Him, because they failed God in what they promised Him and because they lied.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He made hypocrisy follow upon them in their hearts because of their avarice. Whoever breaks his inner pact rejects love at its root; and whoever shows good outwardly while concealing evil inwardly has his share of hypocrisy. The hypocrite is in the last rank in this world, and in the lowest depth of the Fire in the next.

mots.def: discord between the open and the hidden.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ سِرَّهُمْ وَنَجْوَاهُمْ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ
versets.tr: a-lam yaʿlamū anna llāha yaʿlamu sirrahum wa-najwāhum wa-anna llāha ʿallāmu l-ghuyūb
versets.trad: Do they not know that God knows their secret and their private converse, and that God is the All-Knower of unseen things?
themes: the secret and the private converse
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sirr is that over which none rises but God; the najwā is what they whisper to one another. Or by sirr one may understand the thoughts to which their souls have access — God knows all. In his Risāla he specifies: the sirr is the locus of contemplation, as the spirits are the locus of love, and the hearts the locus of gnoses.

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب
mots.def: intensive: knower of all hidden things.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَلْمِزُونَ الْمُطَّوِّعِينَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي الصَّدَقَاتِ وَالَّذِينَ لَا يَجِدُونَ إِلَّا جُهْدَهُمْ فَيَسْخَرُونَ مِنْهُمْ ۙ سَخِرَ اللَّهُ مِنْهُمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: alladhīna yalmizūna l-muṭṭawwiʿīna mina l-muʾminīna fī ṣ-ṣadaqāti wa-lladhīna lā yajidūna illā juhdahum fa-yaskharūna minhum, sakhira llāhu minhum wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīm
versets.trad: Those who slander the believers who give freely in alms, and those who can find only their own effort, mocking them — God shall mock them, and theirs is a painful chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they blamed those whose hands fell short of multiplying alms but who gave what reached them. God thanked the effort of the sincere. And because they wounded the Muslims by their mockery, God describes Himself by what His transcendence truly excludes — mockery — to soothe the hearts of His friends; He is exalted above that in truth, by the might of His Lordship.

mots.def: taṭawwaʿa: to perform a supererogatory act.

versets.ar: اسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ أَوْ لَا تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ إِن تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً فَلَن يَغْفِرَ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَفَرُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: istaghfir lahum aw lā tastaghfir lahum in tastaghfir lahum sabʿīna marratan fa-lan yaghfira llāhu lahum, dhālika bi-annahum kafarū bi-llāhi wa-rasūlih, wa-llāhu lā yahdī l-qawma l-fāsiqīn
versets.trad: Ask forgiveness for them, or do not — if you were to ask it seventy times, God will not forgive them, because they have disbelieved in God and His Messenger. God does not guide a corrupt people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God seals the matters: He does not forgive the people of association and hypocrisy; intercessions profit them not, the fallen among them does not rise. Whoever is overcome by our misfortune, neither his pleading nor his prayer avails him; the felled of Omnipotence is not lifted by effort or contrivance.

versets.ar: فَرِحَ الْمُخَلَّفُونَ بِمَقْعَدِهِمْ خِلَافَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ وَكَرِهُوا أَن يُجَاهِدُوا بِأَمْوَالِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَقَالُوا لَا تَنفِرُوا فِي الْحَرِّ ۗ قُلْ نَارُ جَهَنَّمَ أَشَدُّ حَرًّا ۚ لَّوْ كَانُوا يَفْقَهُونَ
versets.tr: fariḥa l-mukhallafūna bi-maqʿadihim khilāfa rasūli llāhi wa-karihū an yujāhidū bi-amwālihim wa-anfusihim fī sabīli llāhi wa-qālū lā tanfirū fī l-ḥarr, qul nāru jahannama ashaddu ḥarran, law kānū yafqahūn
versets.trad: Those left behind rejoiced at staying behind God's Messenger; they hated to strive with their wealth and persons in God's path, saying: "Do not march out in the heat!" Say: "The fire of Gehenna is fiercer in heat," — if only they understood.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their joy in remaining behind possessed them; they did not know that their ruin lies in their delay, and in what they preferred: the rest of their selves over the fulfilment of God's right, and over departure in the company of the Messenger. God snatched that rest away by His chastisement, and they will burn in the Fire because of the hypocrisy they sent ahead.

versets.ar: فَلْيَضْحَكُوا قَلِيلًا وَلْيَبْكُوا كَثِيرًا جَزَاءً بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: fa-l-yaḍḥakū qalīlan wa-l-yabkū kathīran jazāʾan bi-mā kānū yaksibūn
versets.trad: Let them laugh a little and weep much, in recompense for what they earned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God has exchanged their joy for grief, their rejoicing for sorrow, their rest for sobs; their tears in the hereafter shall multiply as did their laughter in this world — such is the recompense of him who denies his Lord.

versets.ar: فَإِن رَّجَعَكَ اللَّهُ إِلَىٰ طَائِفَةٍ مِّنْهُمْ فَاسْتَأْذَنُوكَ لِلْخُرُوجِ فَقُل لَّن تَخْرُجُوا مَعِيَ أَبَدًا وَلَن تُقَاتِلُوا مَعِيَ عَدُوًّا ۖ إِنَّكُمْ رَضِيتُم بِالْقُعُودِ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ فَاقْعُدُوا مَعَ الْخَالِفِينَ
versets.tr: fa-in rajaʿaka llāhu ilā ṭāʾifatin minhum fa-staʾdhanūka li-l-khurūji fa-qul lan takhrujū maʿiya abadan wa-lan tuqātilū maʿiya ʿaduwwan, innakum raḍītum bi-l-quʿūdi awwala marratin fa-qʿudū maʿa l-khālifīn
versets.trad: If God brings you back to a group of them and they ask leave to go out, say: "You shall never go out with me, nor fight an enemy with me. You were content to stay behind the first time; so sit with those who remain behind."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the thread of the covenant has weakened, it no longer bears to be drawn tight; when the tear has widened, no patch any longer serves.

versets.ar: وَلَا تُصَلِّ عَلَىٰ أَحَدٍ مِّنْهُم مَّاتَ أَبَدًا وَلَا تَقُمْ عَلَىٰ قَبْرِهِ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَفَرُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَمَاتُوا وَهُمْ فَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā tuṣalli ʿalā aḥadin minhum māta abadan wa-lā taqum ʿalā qabrih, innahum kafarū bi-llāhi wa-rasūlihi wa-mātū wa-hum fāsiqūn
versets.trad: Never pray over any of them when he dies, nor stand at his grave: they disbelieved in God and His Messenger, and died as corrupt.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: after dissociation, no alliance; after separation, no accord; after the veil, no nearness. They had a time when hope might find room, when expectation had a margin, when opinion bore meaning; but the decree was written for them in misery — we take refuge in God from a bad end.

mots.def: ṣallā ʿalā: to pray over (the deceased).

versets.ar: وَلَا تُعْجِبْكَ أَمْوَالُهُمْ وَأَوْلَادُهُمْ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ أَن يُعَذِّبَهُم بِهَا فِي الدُّنْيَا وَتَزْهَقَ أَنفُسُهُمْ وَهُمْ كَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā tuʿjibka amwāluhum wa-awlāduhum, innamā yurīdu llāhu an yuʿadhdhibahum bihā fī d-dunyā wa-tazhaqa anfusuhum wa-hum kāfirūn
versets.trad: Let not their wealth or children dazzle you. God only wills to chastise them through these in this world, and that their souls depart while they are unbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: do not think the empowerment given the hypocrites to carry out their will, the multiplying of their goods, is a kindness from Us, or the lavishing of a favour upon them. It is but a stratagem against them, a thread drawn out (istidrāj), respite that is not neglect; soon they shall taste its consequences.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا أُنزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ أَنْ آمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَجَاهِدُوا مَعَ رَسُولِهِ اسْتَأْذَنَكَ أُولُو الطَّوْلِ مِنْهُمْ وَقَالُوا ذَرْنَا نَكُن مَّعَ الْقَاعِدِينَ
