versets.tr: wa-idhā unzilat sūratun an āminū bi-llāhi wa-jāhidū maʿa rasūlihi staʾdhanaka ūlū ṭ-ṭawli minhum wa-qālū dharnā nakun maʿa l-qāʿidīn
versets.trad: When a sūra was sent down [saying]: "Believe in God and strive with His Messenger," the well-off among them asked your leave, saying: "Leave us with those who sit behind."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the command to fight pressed upon them and the obligation grew stern, they pleaded their comfort and inclined to the choice of repose; they contrived obstacles. These God singled out for His abandonment, and turned their hearts from seeking His pleasure.

versets.ar: رَضُوا بِأَن يَكُونُوا مَعَ الْخَوَالِفِ وَطُبِعَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَفْقَهُونَ
versets.tr: raḍū bi-an yakūnū maʿa l-khawālifi wa-ṭubiʿa ʿalā qulūbihim fa-hum lā yafqahūn
versets.trad: They were content to be with those who remained behind, and a seal was set upon their hearts, so they do not understand.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they withdrew from the carpet of worship, they loved repose, they consented to halt in the homelands of separation. Had they returned to God in sincere regret, He would have met them with bounty and generosity; but the decree prevailed, and feigning fell.

versets.ar: لَٰكِنِ الرَّسُولُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ جَاهَدُوا بِأَمْوَالِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ ۚ وَأُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمُ الْخَيْرَاتُ ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: lākini r-rasūlu wa-lladhīna āmanū maʿahu jāhadū bi-amwālihim wa-anfusihim, wa-ūlāʾika lahumu l-khayrātu, wa-ūlāʾika humu l-mufliḥūn
versets.trad: But the Messenger and those who believed with him strove with their wealth and persons: for them are the goods, and they are the successful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who turned toward is not as he who turned away; he who accepted the command is not as he who rejected it; he who unified is not as he who denied; he who worshipped is not as he who persisted; he who came is not as he who refused. No doubt: their trade has profited, their rank has been raised.

versets.ar: أَعَدَّ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: aʿadda llāhu lahum jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā, dhālika l-fawzu l-ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: God has prepared for them Gardens with streams flowing beneath, where they shall abide: that is the supreme triumph!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the verse means that their rest is promised, even if the hardships are at present present and visible. The truth of their certainty in the reward lightens for them the endurance of what they meet.

versets.ar: وَجَاءَ الْمُعَذِّرُونَ مِنَ الْأَعْرَابِ لِيُؤْذَنَ لَهُمْ وَقَعَدَ الَّذِينَ كَذَبُوا اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ۚ سَيُصِيبُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-jāʾa l-muʿadhdhirūna mina l-aʿrābi li-yuʾdhana lahum wa-qaʿada lladhīna kadhabū llāha wa-rasūlah, sa-yuṣību lladhīna kafarū minhum ʿadhābun alīm
versets.trad: Some of the Bedouins offering excuses came asking to be exempted, and those who had lied to God and His Messenger stayed behind. A painful chastisement shall befall those of them who disbelieved.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these are, according to the exegetes, the people of excuses who asked the Messenger to be excused at the Tabūk expedition: the blame fell from them. As for those who hung back without excuse, the blame fell upon them, and for them the threat in the days to come.

versets.ar: لَّيْسَ عَلَى الضُّعَفَاءِ وَلَا عَلَى الْمَرْضَىٰ وَلَا عَلَى الَّذِينَ لَا يَجِدُونَ مَا يُنفِقُونَ حَرَجٌ إِذَا نَصَحُوا لِلَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ۚ مَا عَلَى الْمُحْسِنِينَ مِن سَبِيلٍ ۚ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: laysa ʿalā ḍ-ḍuʿafāʾi wa-lā ʿalā l-marḍā wa-lā ʿalā lladhīna lā yajidūna mā yunfiqūna ḥarajun idhā naṣaḥū lillāhi wa-rasūlih, mā ʿalā l-muḥsinīna min sabīl, wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: No blame falls on the weak, the sick, or those who find nothing to spend, if they are sincere toward God and His Messenger. There is no way against those who do good; God is Forgiving, Merciful,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the value of poverty appears when the command falls: were there in scarcity only this good, this honour, it would suffice. They remained at home, without the command of combat applying to them — He was content with them by the heart's sincere counsel, by the conviction that, had they been able, they would have gone forth. And: the bienfaiteur (muḥsin) is he against whom the Law no longer has any claim, neither in God's right nor in men's.

versets.ar: وَلَا عَلَى الَّذِينَ إِذَا مَا أَتَوْكَ لِتَحْمِلَهُمْ قُلْتَ لَا أَجِدُ مَا أَحْمِلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ تَوَلَّوا وَّأَعْيُنُهُمْ تَفِيضُ مِنَ الدَّمْعِ حَزَنًا أَلَّا يَجِدُوا مَا يُنفِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā ʿalā lladhīna idhā mā atawka li-taḥmilahum qulta lā ajidu mā aḥmilukum ʿalayhi tawallaw wa-aʿyunuhum tafīḍu mina d-damʿi ḥazanan allā yajidū mā yunfiqūn
versets.trad: nor on those who came to you for mounts and to whom you said: "I have nothing to carry you on," and who turned away with eyes overflowing with tears, grieved at finding nothing to spend.
themes: tears of the poor who wished to set forth
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it pained them that the Messenger's heart should be occupied by them; they wished that occupation removed from him — not from inclination toward this world, but lest any displeasure should come to him from them. When the Real knew this, and their hearts were purely attached to God, their conviction emptied of any commerce with a creature, God provided for their state and ordered the Messenger to mount them — such is His custom: "He is the One who sends down rain after they had despaired".

mots.racine: ف ي ض / د م ع

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا السَّبِيلُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَسْتَأْذِنُونَكَ وَهُمْ أَغْنِيَاءُ ۚ رَضُوا بِأَن يَكُونُوا مَعَ الْخَوَالِفِ وَطَبَعَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: innamā s-sabīlu ʿalā lladhīna yastaʾdhinūnaka wa-hum aghniyāʾu, raḍū bi-an yakūnū maʿa l-khawālifi wa-ṭabaʿa llāhu ʿalā qulūbihim fa-hum lā yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: There is blame only upon those who ask leave of you while being rich: content to be with those who stay behind. God has set a seal upon their hearts, so they do not know.
themes: the rich man who hangs back
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the truthful in friendship is not ashamed to bear hardship; the wavering in friendship, the severed from sincerity, alleges what has no foundation, for purity has been denied him there where he ought to have it. Islam praises courage: it is said that God loves bravery, were it only to kill a snake.

versets.ar: يَعْتَذِرُونَ إِلَيْكُمْ إِذَا رَجَعْتُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ ۚ قُل لَّا تَعْتَذِرُوا لَن نُّؤْمِنَ لَكُمْ قَدْ نَبَّأَنَا اللَّهُ مِنْ أَخْبَارِكُمْ ۚ وَسَيَرَى اللَّهُ عَمَلَكُمْ وَرَسُولُهُ ثُمَّ تُرَدُّونَ إِلَىٰ عَالِمِ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: yaʿtadhirūna ilaykum idhā rajaʿtum ilayhim, qul lā taʿtadhirū lan nuʾmina lakum qad nabbaʾanā llāhu min akhbārikum, wa-sa-yarā llāhu ʿamalakum wa-rasūluhu thumma turaddūna ilā ʿālimi l-ghaybi wa-sh-shahādati fa-yunabbiʾukum bi-mā kuntum taʿmalūn
versets.trad: They will excuse themselves to you when you return to them. Say: "Do not excuse yourselves; we shall not believe you. God has already informed us of your tidings. God and His Messenger shall see your deeds; then you shall be brought back to the Knower of the unseen and the visible, who will inform you of what you used to do."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when they say what they lie about, tell them: "God has made known to us your lying; your disgraces have become clear to us; what He has unveiled to us has distinguished your evil from your good. Nothing of your states is hidden from Him; you shall taste the consequence of your works in your tomorrow."

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب / ش ه د
mots.sens: Knower of the unseen and the visible

versets.ar: سَيَحْلِفُونَ بِاللَّهِ لَكُمْ إِذَا انقَلَبْتُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ لِتُعْرِضُوا عَنْهُمْ ۖ فَأَعْرِضُوا عَنْهُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ رِجْسٌ ۖ وَمَأْوَاهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ جَزَاءً بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: sa-yaḥlifūna bi-llāhi lakum idhā nqalabtum ilayhim li-tuʿriḍū ʿanhum, fa-aʿriḍū ʿanhum, innahum rijsun, wa-maʾwāhum jahannamu jazāʾan bi-mā kānū yaksibūn
versets.trad: When you return to them, they will swear to you by God that you might turn away from them. Turn away from them, for they are filth, and their abode is Gehenna, recompense for what they have earned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God grants the disobedient respite till he imagines He has overlooked him — yet this is but a stratagem worked upon him. When he tastes what is due to him, he knows the matter to be quite other than he thought.

versets.ar: يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ لِتَرْضَوْا عَنْهُمْ ۖ فَإِن تَرْضَوْا عَنْهُمْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَرْضَىٰ عَنِ الْقَوْمِ الْفَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: yaḥlifūna lakum li-tarḍaw ʿanhum, fa-in tarḍaw ʿanhum fa-inna llāha lā yarḍā ʿani l-qawmi l-fāsiqīn
versets.trad: They swear to you to win your favour; even if you are pleased with them, God is not pleased with a corrupt people.
themes: God's pleasure, the only real one
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who is the object of the Real's anger derives no profit from being the object of creatures' favour. The stake is not the word of any other than God — the stake is what has preceded for you of felicity in God's decree.

versets.ar: الْأَعْرَابُ أَشَدُّ كُفْرًا وَنِفَاقًا وَأَجْدَرُ أَلَّا يَعْلَمُوا حُدُودَ مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: al-aʿrābu ashaddu kufran wa-nifāqan wa-ajdaru allā yaʿlamū ḥudūda mā anzala llāhu ʿalā rasūlih, wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīm
versets.trad: The Bedouins are more entrenched in disbelief and hypocrisy, and more apt to be ignorant of the bounds God has sent down upon His Messenger. God is Knowing, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their hearts were kneaded in hardness, no onslaught of election came to strike them; they were behind their fellows by the ill of nature; thus they are farther from the clarity of the truths, closer to deserving disgrace.

versets.ar: وَمِنَ الْأَعْرَابِ مَن يَتَّخِذُ مَا يُنفِقُ مَغْرَمًا وَيَتَرَبَّصُ بِكُمُ الدَّوَائِرَ ۚ عَلَيْهِمْ دَائِرَةُ السَّوْءِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-mina l-aʿrābi man yattakhidhu mā yunfiqu maghraman wa-yatarabbaṣu bikumu d-dawāʾir, ʿalayhim dāʾiratu s-sawʾ, wa-llāhu samīʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: Among the Bedouins are those who count what they spend as a loss and watch for misfortunes to fall on you. Upon them shall be the evil reversal: God is Hearing, Knowing.
themes: the reversal that turns upon them
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their convictions were corrupt: they awaited for the Muslims what their base aspirations spelled — the falling of trials. But God would not allow their ruse to fall back upon them. Hence the proverb: "If you dig for your brother, widen the hole: it might well be your own resting place."

versets.ar: وَمِنَ الْأَعْرَابِ مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَيَتَّخِذُ مَا يُنفِقُ قُرُبَاتٍ عِندَ اللَّهِ وَصَلَوَاتِ الرَّسُولِ ۚ أَلَا إِنَّهَا قُرْبَةٌ لَّهُمْ ۚ سَيُدْخِلُهُمُ اللَّهُ فِي رَحْمَتِهِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-mina l-aʿrābi man yuʾminu bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-yattakhidhu mā yunfiqu qurubātin ʿinda llāhi wa-ṣalawāti r-rasūl, a-lā innahā qurbatun lahum, sa-yudkhiluhumu llāhu fī raḥmatih, inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: Among the Bedouins are also those who believe in God and the Last Day, who take what they spend as means of nearness to God and of obtaining the Messenger's prayers. Truly, these are nearness for them: God shall enter them into His mercy. God is Forgiving, Merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they diversified: some cheated and did not profit; some gave sincere counsel and did not lose. Those who tricked are in the precipice of their shame; those who were truthful are in the breath of their beneficence.

versets.ar: وَالسَّابِقُونَ الْأَوَّلُونَ مِنَ الْمُهَاجِرِينَ وَالْأَنصَارِ وَالَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُم بِإِحْسَانٍ رَّضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي تَحْتَهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-s-sābiqūna l-awwalūna mina l-muhājirīna wa-l-anṣāri wa-lladhīna ttabaʿūhum bi-iḥsānin raḍiya llāhu ʿanhum wa-raḍū ʿanhu wa-aʿadda lahum jannātin tajrī taḥtahā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā abadan, dhālika l-fawzu l-ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: The earliest forerunners of the Emigrants and the Helpers, and those who follow them in fair conduct: God is well pleased with them, and they with Him. He has prepared for them Gardens with streams flowing beneath, where they shall abide for ever: that is the supreme triumph!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds: there is he who races ahead by the sincerity of his step, and he who races by the sincerity of his aspirations. And it is said: the forerunner is he whom share has favoured with success, whom decree has filled by realization — God's mercy preceded him. And: He preceded them by His care, then they preceded by their obedience. The pearl he sets: had He not been pleased with them in His pre-eternity, they would never have reached being pleased with Him.

mots.racine: س ب ق / أ و ل

mots.def: those who emigrated from Mecca to Medina.

versets.ar: وَمِمَّنْ حَوْلَكُم مِّنَ الْأَعْرَابِ مُنَافِقُونَ ۖ وَمِنْ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ ۖ مَرَدُوا عَلَى النِّفَاقِ لَا تَعْلَمُهُمْ ۖ نَحْنُ نَعْلَمُهُمْ ۚ سَنُعَذِّبُهُم مَّرَّتَيْنِ ثُمَّ يُرَدُّونَ إِلَىٰ عَذَابٍ عَظِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-mimman ḥawlakum mina l-aʿrābi munāfiqūn, wa-min ahli l-madīnati, maradū ʿalā n-nifāqi lā taʿlamuhum, naḥnu naʿlamuhum, sa-nuʿadhdhibuhum marratayni thumma yuraddūna ilā ʿadhābin ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: Among the Bedouins around you are hypocrites, and among the people of Medina some are hardened in hypocrisy. You do not know them; We know them. We shall chastise them twice, then they shall be brought to a grievous chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sincere and the hypocrite resemble each other by form: they are not distinguished by appearances, though they oppose each other in truths and meanings. Their knowledge could not reach this finesse — God tore open their veils for His Prophet, made them known to him; while they were ignorant that He saw them, remaining fixed in the homelands of their duplicity, to whom the length of respite did not profit.

mots.def: trials of this world then of the grave.

versets.ar: وَآخَرُونَ اعْتَرَفُوا بِذُنُوبِهِمْ خَلَطُوا عَمَلًا صَالِحًا وَآخَرَ سَيِّئًا عَسَى اللَّهُ أَن يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-ākharūna ʿtarafū bi-dhunūbihim khalaṭū ʿamalan ṣāliḥan wa-ākhara sayyiʾan ʿasā llāhu an yatūba ʿalayhim, inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: And others have confessed their sins; they have mingled a righteous deed with another that is evil. It may be that God will turn to them in mercy: God is Forgiving, Merciful.
themes: the mingling of good and evil
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if they have marked themselves with their faults, at least they have confessed their sins. Confession binds rights among men; but confession before God — by the custom of His generosity — entails the wiping of the fault. "I was told: 'So-and-so harmed you'; I answered: 'He came to me with a fair excuse — the price of fault, with us, is the excuse offered.'" And: "It may be" indicates that nothing is binding upon God: He may turn, He may not turn; but His word is truthful: if the promise is made, it is fulfilled.

versets.ar: خُذْ مِنْ أَمْوَالِهِمْ صَدَقَةً تُطَهِّرُهُمْ وَتُزَكِّيهِم بِهَا وَصَلِّ عَلَيْهِمْ ۖ إِنَّ صَلَاتَكَ سَكَنٌ لَّهُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: khudh min amwālihim ṣadaqatan tuṭahhiruhum wa-tuzakkīhim bihā wa-ṣalli ʿalayhim, inna ṣalātaka sakanun lahum, wa-llāhu samīʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: Take from their wealth an alms by which to cleanse and purify them, and pray for them: your prayer is a comfort for them. God is Hearing, Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "it purifies them" of the quest of recompense for their gifts, "it sanctifies them" of the attachment of their gaze to themselves. It purifies them of the avarice of their souls, sanctifies them of self-sufficiency through their wealth — that they may see God's great grace: the finding of detachment. "Your prayer is comfort (sakan) for them" — your companionship in aspiration is more precious to them than their independence by their fortune.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ يَقْبَلُ التَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبَادِهِ وَيَأْخُذُ الصَّدَقَاتِ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: a-lam yaʿlamū anna llāha huwa yaqbalu t-tawbata ʿan ʿibādihi wa-yaʾkhudhu ṣ-ṣadaqāti wa-anna llāha huwa t-tawwābu r-raḥīm
versets.trad: Do they not know that it is God who accepts repentance from His servants and receives the alms, and that God is the Acceptor of repentance, the Merciful?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He praised Himself for accepting the repentance of the disobedient, for through it His generosity appears; as He praised Himself for the splendour of His might, that one may know His pre-eternity. And as He is alone in His singular splendour, He is alone in accepting repentance. Small or great, the alms — its worth depends upon His acceptance, not on its quantity.

mots.racine: ق ب ل / ت و ب

mots.sens: the One who ever returns to His servant

versets.ar: وَقُلِ اعْمَلُوا فَسَيَرَى اللَّهُ عَمَلَكُمْ وَرَسُولُهُ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ ۖ وَسَتُرَدُّونَ إِلَىٰ عَالِمِ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-quli ʿmalū fa-sa-yarā llāhu ʿamalakum wa-rasūluhu wa-l-muʾminūn, wa-sa-turaddūna ilā ʿālimi l-ghaybi wa-sh-shahādati fa-yunabbiʾukum bi-mā kuntum taʿmalūn
versets.trad: Say: "Work! God will see your work, His Messenger and the believers. Then you shall be brought back to the Knower of the unseen and the visible, who will inform you of what you used to do."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He frightened them by His seeing their deeds. Knowing that there are among them those whose state is too short for shame at the Real's knowledge, He added: "and His Messenger"; then for those whose rank descends further: "and the believers." Lost is he whom no modesty restrains — he has fallen from God's eye.

versets.ar: وَآخَرُونَ مُرْجَوْنَ لِأَمْرِ اللَّهِ إِمَّا يُعَذِّبُهُمْ وَإِمَّا يَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-ākharūna murjawna li-amri llāhi immā yuʿadhdhibuhum wa-immā yatūbu ʿalayhim, wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīm
versets.trad: And others await God's command: either He will chastise them or turn to them. God is Knowing, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets a pearl of equilibrium: He did not declare their repentance accepted, nor marked them with despair of His pardon. They stand on the foot of shame, swaying between reverence and desire, hesitating between fear and hope. "If He chastises them, no objection reaches Him; if He has mercy, none can prevent Him." — "The promise sates me with hope; my knowledge of my failing, with menace."

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا مَسْجِدًا ضِرَارًا وَكُفْرًا وَتَفْرِيقًا بَيْنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَإِرْصَادًا لِّمَنْ حَارَبَ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ وَلَيَحْلِفُنَّ إِنْ أَرَدْنَا إِلَّا الْحُسْنَىٰ ۖ وَاللَّهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّهُمْ لَكَاذِبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna ttakhadhū masjidan ḍirāran wa-kufran wa-tafrīqan bayna l-muʾminīna wa-irṣādan li-man ḥāraba llāha wa-rasūlahu min qabl, wa-la-yaḥlifunna in aradnā illā l-ḥusnā, wa-llāhu yashhadu innahum la-kādhibūn
versets.trad: Those who built a mosque to do harm, in disbelief, to divide the believers, and as an outpost for him who had warred against God and His Messenger — they will surely swear: "We intended only good!" But God bears witness that they are liars.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who is not sincere in his alliance, his heart does not relish the toil and effort; his outward tenderness cries out the twist of his depth, and his speech kneaded with contrivance bears truthful witness to the absence of his purity.

versets.ar: لَا تَقُمْ فِيهِ أَبَدًا ۚ لَّمَسْجِدٌ أُسِّسَ عَلَى التَّقْوَىٰ مِنْ أَوَّلِ يَوْمٍ أَحَقُّ أَن تَقُومَ فِيهِ ۚ فِيهِ رِجَالٌ يُحِبُّونَ أَن يَتَطَهَّرُوا ۚ وَاللَّهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُطَّهِّرِينَ
versets.tr: lā taqum fīhi abadan, la-masjidun ussisa ʿalā t-taqwā min awwali yawmin aḥaqqu an taqūma fīhi, fīhi rijālun yuḥibbūna an yataṭahharū, wa-llāhu yuḥibbu l-muṭṭahhirīn
versets.trad: Never stand in it. A mosque founded on godfearing from the very first day has more right that you stand in it. In it are men who love to purify themselves; God loves those who purify themselves.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the degrees: "in it are men who love to purify themselves" — they purify themselves from disobedience: this is the mark of adorers; they purify themselves from passions and hopes: this is the quality of renunciants; they purify themselves from love of creatures, then from the sight of themselves in their attributes: this is the quality of gnostics.

versets.ar: أَفَمَنْ أَسَّسَ بُنْيَانَهُ عَلَىٰ تَقْوَىٰ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَرِضْوَانٍ خَيْرٌ أَم مَّنْ أَسَّسَ بُنْيَانَهُ عَلَىٰ شَفَا جُرُفٍ هَارٍ فَانْهَارَ بِهِ فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: a-fa-man assasa bunyānahu ʿalā taqwā mina llāhi wa-riḍwānin khayrun am man assasa bunyānahu ʿalā shafā jurufin hārin fa-nhāra bihi fī nāri jahannam, wa-llāhu lā yahdī l-qawma ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: Is he who founded his building on godfearing from God and His good pleasure better — or he who founded his building on the edge of a crumbling cliff that collapses with him into the fire of Gehenna? God does not guide a people of wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives here a whole spiritual programme: the murīd must found his building on the truthfulness of his certainty in what he believes; then on the purity of resolve not to deviate from the road before reaching the goal; then on the uprooting of all his aspirations, desires, aims; then on the permanence of dhikr without forgetfulness creeping in; then on the observance of the Muslim brothers' right, preferring others to himself. He who ruins the foundations at the start is denied arrival at the end; he who has not secured the base — the roof will fall upon his walls.

mots.racine: ش ف و / ج ر ف / ه و ر
mots.sens: the edge of a crumbling cliff
mots.def: shafā: edge; juruf: ravine; hār: collapsing.

versets.ar: لَا يَزَالُ بُنْيَانُهُمُ الَّذِي بَنَوْا رِيبَةً فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ إِلَّا أَن تَقَطَّعَ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: lā yazālu bunyānuhumu lladhī banaw rībatan fī qulūbihim illā an taqaṭṭaʿa qulūbuhum, wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīm
versets.trad: The building they raised will never cease to be a source of doubt in their hearts, unless their hearts be torn to pieces. God is Knowing, Wise.
themes: the doubt that will not be uprooted
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the roots of hypocrisy are not uprooted from the courtyards of certainty save by the scythe of verifying the sound proof. He who is helped to persevere on the path and aided to ponder the proof reaches the coolness of the breast and the breath of gnosis. He who remains at the routine of imitation, his heart never rests from the toil of hesitation, the darkness of conjecture, the swirl of troubled thoughts.

mots.sens: that their hearts be torn apart

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ اشْتَرَىٰ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَأَمْوَالَهُم بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ الْجَنَّةَ ۚ يُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَيَقْتُلُونَ وَيُقْتَلُونَ ۖ وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّا فِي التَّوْرَاةِ وَالْإِنجِيلِ وَالْقُرْآنِ ۚ وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِ مِنَ اللَّهِ ۚ فَاسْتَبْشِرُوا بِبَيْعِكُمُ الَّذِي بَايَعْتُم بِهِ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: inna llāha shtarā mina l-muʾminīna anfusahum wa-amwālahum bi-anna lahumu l-jannah, yuqātilūna fī sabīli llāhi fa-yaqtulūna wa-yuqtalūn, waʿdan ʿalayhi ḥaqqan fī t-tawrāti wa-l-injīli wa-l-qurʾān, wa-man awfā bi-ʿahdihi mina llāh, fa-stabshirū bi-bayʿikumu lladhī bāyaʿtum bih, wa-dhālika huwa l-fawzu l-ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: God has bought from the believers their persons and their wealth, in exchange for the Garden. They fight in God's path: they slay and are slain — a true promise binding upon Him in the Torah, the Gospel, and the Qurʾān. And who is more faithful to his covenant than God? Rejoice then in the bargain you have struck: that is the supreme triumph!
themes: the pact of the three Books
versets.commentaire: Pivotal verse. Qushayrī: "He has bought from the believers their persons" — He did not say "their hearts." For the nafs is the seat of defects; He has set the Garden against it. The heart, by contrast, is worth more than the Garden: its price is what He reserves for His friends in the Garden — the precious vision of His face. The nafs is the seat of defect, and the Generous desires to buy what others turn away from. And his master al-Daqqāq said: "He did not say 'He has bought their hearts,' for the hearts are a waqf consecrated to His love, and a waqf is not sold." And: "Rejoice in your sale" — He called bayʿ what in truth was only His purchase: "You did not cast when you cast, but God cast" — this is the very source of the Gathering.

mots.racine: ن ف س / م و ل

mots.sens: the Torah, the Gospel, the Qurʾān

versets.ar: التَّائِبُونَ الْعَابِدُونَ الْحَامِدُونَ السَّائِحُونَ الرَّاكِعُونَ السَّاجِدُونَ الْآمِرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَالنَّاهُونَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ وَالْحَافِظُونَ لِحُدُودِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: at-tāʾibūna l-ʿābidūna l-ḥāmidūna s-sāʾiḥūna r-rākiʿūna s-sājidūna l-āmirūna bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-n-nāhūna ʿani l-munkari wa-l-ḥāfiẓūna li-ḥudūdi llāh, wa-bashshiri l-muʾminīn
versets.trad: They are those who repent, who worship, who praise, who journey [in piety], who bow, who prostrate, who enjoin right and forbid wrong, and who keep the bounds of God. Give glad tidings to the believers!
themes: the nine qualities of the believers
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds each attribute by degrees. Of the tāʾibūn: there is he who returns from fault to obedience; he who returns from following passion to the accord of His good pleasure; he who returns from the seeing of self to the seeing of His subtlety; he who returns from the sensing of self and his kind to immersion in the truths of His right. Of the sāʾiḥūn: they fast — not from food — but from the sight of every other than God; they journey by their secrets in the Kingdom, until they find the breath of union, and live by the breath of intimacy with the Real. And on the sājidūn: prostration has its degrees — by sound intention, one prostrates in lowliness on the carpet of need; by contemplation, the heart prostrates in the Real's presence and has no other gaze than Him; by union, it is the very extinction of the sense of self.

mots.racine: ر ك ع / س ج د
mots.sens: those who bow, those who prostrate

mots.racine: ح ف ظ / ح د د

versets.ar: مَا كَانَ لِلنَّبِيِّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَن يَسْتَغْفِرُوا لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ وَلَوْ كَانُوا أُولِي قُرْبَىٰ مِن بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُمْ أَصْحَابُ الْجَحِيمِ
versets.tr: mā kāna li-n-nabiyyi wa-lladhīna āmanū an yastaghfirū li-l-mushrikīna wa-law kānū ūlī qurbā min baʿdi mā tabayyana lahum annahum aṣḥābu l-jaḥīm
versets.trad: It is not for the Prophet and the believers to ask forgiveness for the idolaters, even if they be close kin, once it has become clear that they are companions of the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the foundation of religion is dissociation from enemies and alliance with friends; and the friend has no near kin, no intimate, no relative, no companion — if he allies himself, it is by command; if he opposes, it is by prohibition.

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ اسْتِغْفَارُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ إِلَّا عَن مَّوْعِدَةٍ وَعَدَهَا إِيَّاهُ فَلَمَّا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ أَنَّهُ عَدُوٌّ لِّلَّهِ تَبَرَّأَ مِنْهُ ۚ إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لَأَوَّاهٌ حَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna istighfāru ibrāhīma li-abīhi illā ʿan mawʿidatin waʿadahā iyyāhu fa-lammā tabayyana lahu annahu ʿaduwwun lillāhi tabarraʾa minh, inna ibrāhīma la-awwāhun ḥalīm
versets.trad: Abraham's seeking forgiveness for his father was only because of a promise he had made; but when it became clear to him that he was an enemy of God, he dissociated himself from him. Abraham was indeed soft-hearted and forbearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when He commanded the Muslims to dissociate themselves from the idolaters and to refrain from asking forgiveness for them, He pointed out that this is the way of the friends and the path of the prophets. Abraham, if he had asked forgiveness for his father, did so before he was certain that he would not believe; when he knew him to be an enemy of God, he manifested his dissociation.

mots.racine: أ و ه / ح ل م
mots.def: awwāh: one who sighs in humility; ḥalīm: indulgent.

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيُضِلَّ قَوْمًا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَاهُمْ حَتَّىٰ يُبَيِّنَ لَهُم مَّا يَتَّقُونَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna llāhu li-yuḍilla qawman baʿda idh hadāhum ḥattā yubayyina lahum mā yattaqūn, inna llāha bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīm
versets.trad: God does not lead a people astray after having guided them, until He has made plain to them what they should guard against. God knows all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: His gift is not withdrawn save by a breach of courtesy from you. He whom the carpet of union had received will not be struck afterward by the chastisement of separation, except for a rupture of the sanctity.

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ ۚ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ مِن وَلِيٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ
versets.tr: inna llāha lahu mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍ, yuḥyī wa-yumīt, wa-mā lakum min dūni llāhi min waliyyin wa-lā naṣīr
versets.trad: Truly God's is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth: He gives life and gives death. You have, besides God, no protector or helper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He gives life to the hearts of gnostics by the lights of unions; He gives death to the souls of adorers by the marks of descents. He gives life to the one who turns to Him through His bounty; He gives death to the one who turns away through His Majesty.

mots.sens: the kingdom of heavens and earth

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.def: two contrary acts under one will.

versets.ar: لَّقَد تَّابَ اللَّهُ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ وَالْمُهَاجِرِينَ وَالْأَنصَارِ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ فِي سَاعَةِ الْعُسْرَةِ مِن بَعْدِ مَا كَادَ يَزِيغُ قُلُوبُ فَرِيقٍ مِّنْهُمْ ثُمَّ تَابَ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ إِنَّهُ بِهِمْ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: laqad tāba llāhu ʿalā n-nabiyyi wa-l-muhājirīna wa-l-anṣāri lladhīna ttabaʿūhu fī sāʿati l-ʿusrati min baʿdi mā kāda yazīghu qulūbu farīqin minhum thumma tāba ʿalayhim, innahu bihim raʾūfun raḥīm
versets.trad: God has turned in mercy to the Prophet, to the Emigrants and to the Helpers who followed him in the hour of hardship, after the hearts of a group of them had nearly swerved. Then He turned to them: truly He is to them most kind, most merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when His friends are on the brink of foundering, when they draw near to perdition, when despair settles in their hearts and they brace to taste the trial — He rains upon them the clouds of His generosity; the branch of life after its drying becomes green again, the water of intimacy after its drying gushes fresh and quivering.

mots.racine: س و ع / ع س ر
ر أ ف / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَعَلَى الثَّلَاثَةِ الَّذِينَ خُلِّفُوا حَتَّىٰ إِذَا ضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَرْضُ بِمَا رَحُبَتْ وَضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ أَنفُسُهُمْ وَظَنُّوا أَن لَّا مَلْجَأَ مِنَ اللَّهِ إِلَّا إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ تَابَ عَلَيْهِمْ لِيَتُوبُوا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-ʿalā th-thalāthati lladhīna khullifū ḥattā idhā ḍāqat ʿalayhimu l-arḍu bi-mā raḥubat wa-ḍāqat ʿalayhim anfusuhum wa-ẓannū an lā maljaʾa mina llāhi illā ilayh, thumma tāba ʿalayhim li-yatūbū, inna llāha huwa t-tawwābu r-raḥīm
versets.trad: And [He also turned] to the three who were left behind, until the earth, vast as it was, became narrow for them, their own souls became narrow upon them, and they thought there was no refuge from God except in Him. Then He turned to them that they might turn to Him. Truly God is the Acceptor of repentance, the Merciful.
themes: "no refuge from God except in Him"
the return that calls forth return
versets.commentaire: Summit-verse of the sūra. Qushayrī, lapidary: when their refuge was truly sincere, He met them with healing and lifted the trial. Thus the Real rolls the day of ease over the nights of hardship; He makes the suns of trial rise upon the gloom of sedition, and turns the sphere of felicity, abolishing the trace of fortune's blows — His unchanging way, His custom of generosity that He maintains without alteration. The central pearl: "there is no refuge from God except in Him" — the flight reaches Him only from Him.

mots.racine: ض ي ق / أ ر ض / ر ح ب
mots.sens: the earth, vast as it was, narrowed upon them

mots.ar: لَا مَلْجَأَ مِنَ اللَّهِ إِلَّا إِلَيْهِ
mots.tr: lā maljaʾa mina llāhi illā ilayh
mots.sens: no refuge from God save toward Him

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَكُونُوا مَعَ الصَّادِقِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū ttaqū llāha wa-kūnū maʿa ṣ-ṣādiqīn
versets.trad: O you who believe, be mindful of God, and be with the truthful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: truthfulness (ṣidq), as it is in words, is in states — and this is its most complete share. It is the equality of secret and apparent — and that is rare. It is said in the Psalms: "He lied who claimed to love Me, and who, when night enveloped him, slept far from Me".

versets.ar: مَا كَانَ لِأَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهُم مِّنَ الْأَعْرَابِ أَن يَتَخَلَّفُوا عَن رَّسُولِ اللَّهِ وَلَا يَرْغَبُوا بِأَنفُسِهِمْ عَن نَّفْسِهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ لَا يُصِيبُهُمْ ظَمَأٌ وَلَا نَصَبٌ وَلَا مَخْمَصَةٌ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَلَا يَطَئُونَ مَوْطِئًا يَغِيظُ الْكُفَّارَ وَلَا يَنَالُونَ مِنْ عَدُوٍّ نَّيْلًا إِلَّا كُتِبَ لَهُم بِهِ عَمَلٌ صَالِحٌ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: mā kāna li-ahli l-madīnati wa-man ḥawlahum mina l-aʿrābi an yatakhallafū ʿan rasūli llāhi wa-lā yarghabū bi-anfusihim ʿan nafsih, dhālika bi-annahum lā yuṣībuhum ẓamaʾun wa-lā naṣabun wa-lā makhmaṣatun fī sabīli llāhi wa-lā yaṭaʾūna mawṭiʾan yaghīẓu l-kuffāra wa-lā yanālūna min ʿaduwwin naylan illā kutiba lahum bihi ʿamalun ṣāliḥ, inna llāha lā yuḍīʿu ajra l-muḥsinīn
versets.trad: It does not befit the people of Medina and the Bedouins around them to lag behind God's Messenger, or to prefer their own selves to his. For not a thirst, fatigue, or hunger befalls them in God's path, nor do they tread a place that enrages the unbelievers, nor inflict harm on an enemy, without a righteous work being inscribed for them. God does not let the reward of those who do good go to waste.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it is not permitted to them to prefer to the Prophet anything of their person, breath, wealth, child, family — and they lose by this nothing before God; how could they? They do not raise a step for Him without His giving them a thousand steps; they do not place a foot for Him without His meeting them with grace and generosity; they do not bear for Him a thirst without His giving them to drink a cup of His loves.

versets.ar: وَلَا يُنفِقُونَ نَفَقَةً صَغِيرَةً وَلَا كَبِيرَةً وَلَا يَقْطَعُونَ وَادِيًا إِلَّا كُتِبَ لَهُمْ لِيَجْزِيَهُمُ اللَّهُ أَحْسَنَ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā yunfiqūna nafaqatan ṣaghīratan wa-lā kabīratan wa-lā yaqṭaʿūna wādiyan illā kutiba lahum li-yajziyahumu llāhu aḥsana mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: They spend no expenditure, small or great, and cross no valley, without it being inscribed for them, that God may reward them with the best of what they have done.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: nothing of their gift is lost, nothing of their toil wasted — God thanks the effort of the good and pays them back with the best of what they have done.

mots.racine: ن ف ق / ص غ ر / ك ب ر

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ لِيَنفِرُوا كَافَّةً ۚ فَلَوْلَا نَفَرَ مِن كُلِّ فِرْقَةٍ مِّنْهُمْ طَائِفَةٌ لِّيَتَفَقَّهُوا فِي الدِّينِ وَلِيُنذِرُوا قَوْمَهُمْ إِذَا رَجَعُوا إِلَيْهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَحْذَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna l-muʾminūna li-yanfirū kāffatan, fa-law-lā nafara min kulli firqatin minhum ṭāʾifatun li-yatafaqqahū fī d-dīni wa-li-yundhirū qawmahum idhā rajaʿū ilayhim laʿallahum yaḥdharūn
versets.trad: It is not for the believers to go forth all together. Why does not a contingent from every group of them go forth, to gain understanding in religion and warn their people when they return to them, so that they may take heed?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ranks the Muslims by orders: the commonalty are like the king's subjects; the transmitters of hadith like the king's treasurers; the people of the Qurʾān like the keepers of the registers and treasuries; the fuqahāʾ like the king's deputies; the theologians like the commanders of armies; the saints (awliyāʾ) like the pillars of the door; the people of the heart, the companions of purity, like the intimates and table-companions of the king. Some guard the pillars of the Law; others execute the judgements; others reply to opponents; others enjoin good and forbid evil; and a people apart — the men of contemplation — have no office: they guard their breaths with God.

mots.racine: ن ف ر / ك ف ف
mots.sens: that they march forth en masse
that they gain understanding in religion
mots.def: to acquire the science of dīn.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قَاتِلُوا الَّذِينَ يَلُونَكُم مِّنَ الْكُفَّارِ وَلْيَجِدُوا فِيكُمْ غِلْظَةً ۚ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū qātilū lladhīna yalūnakum mina l-kuffāri wa-l-yajidū fīkum ghilẓah, wa-ʿlamū anna llāha maʿa l-muttaqīn
versets.trad: O you who believe, fight those of the unbelievers who are near to you, and let them find harshness in you. Know that God is with the godfearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī shifts at once to the inner: the closest enemy to the Muslim among the unbelievers — the one he must fight first — is the most implacable enemy, his self. He must begin by fighting his self, then fight the unbelievers. The Prophet said: "We have returned from the lesser jihād to the greater jihād."

versets.ar: وَإِذَا مَا أُنزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ فَمِنْهُم مَّن يَقُولُ أَيُّكُمْ زَادَتْهُ هَٰذِهِ إِيمَانًا ۚ فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَزَادَتْهُمْ إِيمَانًا وَهُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā mā unzilat sūratun fa-minhum man yaqūlu ayyukum zādathu hādhihi īmānan, fa-ammā lladhīna āmanū fa-zādathum īmānan wa-hum yastabshirūn
versets.trad: When a sūra is sent down, some of them say: "Which of you has had his faith increased by this?" As for those who believe, it has increased their faith, and they rejoice.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God has made the descent of the Qurʾān a healing for one people and a misery for another. For the believers, the sūra increases faith: they rise from the gaze of proof to the breath of clear utterance; then from clear utterance to contemplation (ʿiyān); hesitation, irresolution, perplexity are then driven from their hearts; the suns of gnosis rise upon their secrets, the lights of verification take possession of them.

versets.ar: وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ فَزَادَتْهُمْ رِجْسًا إِلَىٰ رِجْسِهِمْ وَمَاتُوا وَهُمْ كَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ammā lladhīna fī qulūbihim maraḍun fa-zādathum rijsan ilā rijsihim wa-mātū wa-hum kāfirūn
versets.trad: But as for those in whose hearts is sickness, it adds defilement to their defilement, and they die as unbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: for them the new sūra increases their doubt and perplexity; they enquire of one another about their state, and grow only in consternation, as the verse says: "for them it is blindness".

versets.ar: أَوَلَا يَرَوْنَ أَنَّهُمْ يُفْتَنُونَ فِي كُلِّ عَامٍ مَّرَّةً أَوْ مَرَّتَيْنِ ثُمَّ لَا يَتُوبُونَ وَلَا هُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lā yarawna annahum yuftanūna fī kulli ʿāmin marratan aw marratayni thumma lā yatūbūna wa-lā hum yadhdhakkarūn
versets.trad: Do they not see that they are tested once or twice every year, yet they do not repent and do not take heed?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Real has not left the bearers of the charge without signs of knowing. But as for the people of realities (ahl al-ḥaqāʾiq), while for the others the trial is once or twice a year, for them it is at every breath: He never leaves them without reminders that summon clear sight, without thoughts that contain commands and precepts.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا مَا أُنزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ نَّظَرَ بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ هَلْ يَرَاكُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ ثُمَّ انصَرَفُوا ۚ صَرَفَ اللَّهُ قُلُوبَهُم بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَفْقَهُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā mā unzilat sūratun naẓara baʿḍuhum ilā baʿḍin hal yarākum min aḥadin thumma nṣarafū, ṣarafa llāhu qulūbahum bi-annahum qawmun lā yafqahūn
versets.trad: When a sūra is sent down, they glance at one another: "Is anyone watching you?" Then they turn away. May God turn their hearts away, for they are a people who do not understand.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they veiled themselves with the veil of duplicity, thinking they would remain in secret by their contrivance; but the Real would not but expose them: as He marked them with the seal of denial, He informed the secrets of the men of Tawḥīd of their states, who knew them as they were.

mots.racine: ص ر ف / ق ل ب

versets.ar: لَقَدْ جَاءَكُمْ رَسُولٌ مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ عَزِيزٌ عَلَيْهِ مَا عَنِتُّمْ حَرِيصٌ عَلَيْكُم بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: laqad jāʾakum rasūlun min anfusikum ʿazīzun ʿalayhi mā ʿanittum ḥarīṣun ʿalaykum bi-l-muʾminīna raʾūfun raḥīm
versets.trad: There has come to you a Messenger from your own, to whom your hardship is grievous, who is concerned for you, and toward the believers most kind, most merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: a Messenger has come to you, resembling you in human nature; but when We singled him out by Our election, We clothed him in the garment of mercy toward you, set him up as witness of tenderness and pity toward you; his every aspiration turns to your concern, his greatest care is your faith.

versets.ar: فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَقُلْ حَسْبِيَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ۖ وَهُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ
versets.tr: fa-in tawallaw fa-qul ḥasbiya llāhu lā ilāha illā huw, ʿalayhi tawakkalt, wa-huwa rabbu l-ʿarshi l-ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: If they turn away, say: "God suffices me. There is no god but He. In Him I trust. He is the Lord of the immense Throne."
versets.commentaire: Closing verse, seal of the sūra. Qushayrī: He commanded him to call creatures to Unicity; then: if they turn away from responding, be with Us by the mark of dispossession. It is also said: He told him "O Prophet, God suffices you"; then He commanded him to say "God suffices me." This is the source of the Gathering (ʿayn al-jamʿ); "say: God suffices me" is differentiation — rather, the Gathering of the Gathering (jamʿ al-jamʿ) — that is, say, but it is by Us that you say, and We are the speaker through you; you are annihilated in the very source of Tawḥīd, so you are by Us, effaced from all but Us.

mots.sens: there is no god but He

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ع ر ش / ع ظ م
